definition: (Latin) From the beginning; used to describe contracts, marriages, deeds, etc., that are either valid or void from their inception.
American Bar Association.
To intentionally give up a right or property without any plan of reclaiming it in the future; to desert a spouse or child. N. abandonment.
(1) A homeless person. (2) A boat abandoned on the sea whose master and crew are not expected to return.
A nuisance that can be reduced.
To decrease, reduce, or diminish; to end, dismiss, or temporarily suspend a lawsuit. N.
abatable.
A reduction in the amount of a legacy after the payment of debts owed by the person who granted the legacy.
A reduction in the amount owed by the taxee.

To renounce a responsibility or position; generally used to describe the act of a sovereign giving up a throne or an official renouncing the privileges and duties of his or her office; also used to describe a government or official failing to fulfill responsibilities or duties. N. abdication.

(1) To take or carry away a person illegally by force or persuasion. (2) To take away or detain unlawfully a female, intending to force her into marriage, concubinage, or prostitution. (3) For a woman to entice a husband to abandon his wife for her. N. abduction. See also kidnap, alienation of affections.
To help or encourage someone else to commit a crime.
A person who abets or instigates a crime; an abettor shares criminal intent with the person who in fact commits the crime. See also aid and abet, accessory, accomplice.
A state of temporary disuse or suspension; an unsettled state; the condition of an estate in fee or freehold during a lapse in succession with no current titleholder or owner.
severability.

term: Able to be divided or separated
definition: separability.
marketability.
A person or device that starts fires.
Anti-ballistic missile.
A home or place of residence; a domicile.
To end or do away with; generally used to describe formally ending an institution, system, or custom, such as slavery or a tax. N. abolition.
Indigenous, native to a place from earliest times.
The exclusive rights of American Indians to lands and waters they occupied before the United States claimed them.

The premature ending of a pregnancy; in legal context, generally refers to a deliberate termination, though the term can also apply to spontaneous natural expulsion of a fetus before it becomes viable. V. abort.
Someone who performs abortions.
Approximately; near in time, degree, or quality.
Selling for more than face value. par, at. ADJ. Selling at face value, when face value and market value are equal. par,
To shorten or condense while retaining the sense of the original document. N. abridgement.
To repeal, revoke, or end; particularly applies to laws, rights, orders, or formal agreements. N. abrogation.
To depart quickly and secretly in order to avoid arrest or a lawsuit, particularly after committing some crime such as theft; to leave the jurisdiction of local courts or to hide from them.
A bequest that comes into effect immediately without any conditions or restrictions. legacy,
To refrain from doing something, such as voting. N. abstention.
A policy that allows federal courts to relinquish jurisdiction over a matter and allow a state court to decide a federal constitutional question or a matter of state law.

Existing only in thought or theory and not in reality N. (1) A summary, abridgement, or condensation of a longer document. (2) That which is abstract or theoretical, often used in the phrase "in the abstract." V. (1) To summarize or abridge. (2) To remove something from something else, as in abstracting money from a bank. N. abstraction.
A short summary of the history of a case taken from the trial court record to show an appellate court what issues were considered at trial.
A short history of a piece of land tracing title through all conveyances, transfers, and liens or encumbrances; used to prove an owner's right to the property.
Illogical, incongruous, and obviously untrue. N. absurdity.
To misuse; to wrong or mistreat a person or animal physically, mentally, or sexually. N. Improper use; corrupt acts; cruel treatment of another; violence or sexual assault toward another. abuse,
A decision or judgment made by a trial court that is inappropriate, inconsistent with the facts of the matter, and not according to precedent or established law.
Using the courts and legal process for some improper purpose, such as initiating a lawsuit for revenge or intimidation.
To share a boundary; to touch; to adjoin.
To go faster; to increase; to shorten the time in which a future event will occur, particularly used to discuss the vesting of property rights. N. acceleration.
A method of depreciation that provides larger deductions in the earlier years of an asset's life. depreciation,
An accounting method that writes off the cost of a fixed asset over a period of time. See also cash method. ABBRV. ACRS.

A clause in a contract or mortgage that causes the entire payment to become due if a specific event occurs; common in installment contracts, in which the entire debt becomes due if the buyer fails to make payments on time.
To receive willingly; to agree voluntarily; implies the right to refuse.
validity. See also void.
In contract law, voluntarily consenting to an offer, which then creates a binding contract. acceptance,
An opportunity to do or use something; a means of approach or communication; an opportunity for sexual intercourse; a property owner's right to go to and from his or her land.

(1) An addition; something added to an existing body of property; the right to ownership of one's property even after its form has been altered (e.g., if A cuts down B's tree and makes it into a chair, B can still claim ownership of the chair by accession). (2) The attainment of a rank or title, as in a monarch's accession to a throne. (3) Accepting or joining a treaty or association. V. accede.
(1) Something added to another object as a decoration or to make it more useful. (2) Someone who helps another person commit a crime.
A person who aids a felon, knowing that he or she has committed a felony and intending to help him or her escape punishment. See also accomplice, aid and abet, conspiracy.
Someone who encourages or helps another plan to commit a felony but who is not present during the commission of the crime.
A chance occurrence or incident; an unforeseen and unintended event; often used to describe unfortunate occurrences, such as injury or mishap. ADJ. accidental. accident,
To meet someone's wishes or demands; to adapt to; to do something as a favor, without consideration. N. accommodation.
An indorsement made without consideration for the benefit of the person holding the instrument,
A loan given without any consideration or security out of friendship or the lender's desire to aid the borrower.
A note signed by an accommodation maker. Also called accommodation paper.

A person who signs his or her name to a note or commercial paper to lend credit to another signer, the accommodated party, without consideration and thereby assuming liability for the debt; a cosigner or surety.
Someone who knowingly and willingly helps another commit a crime. See also aid and abet, accessory.
To give or grant; to agree. N. An agreement; a treaty; an agreement between two parties that settles a dispute and provides satisfaction to the wronged party.

A means of ending a dispute by forming an agreement (the accord) that one party will pay the other some consideration (the satisfaction, often less than the amount originally agreed to) and that this will discharge any remaining obligation.
A kind of interest-bearing checking account.
To provide a satisfactory explanation for something.
Debts owed by a business.
Money owed to a business.
Responsibility; the state of being answerable for something. accountant. A person trained in accounting, bookkeeping, and taxes. accountant,
(1) A system of keeping financial records. (2) An action in equity to settle the finances of a partnership.
The method used by a business to calculate its income and expenditures for tax purposes. See also accrual method, cash method.
To recognize officially or authorize; to attribute; to send someone to another place (often internationally) as an official envoy.
To grow or increase by accumulation.

(1) Growing or adding on to something; typically used to describe natural growth such as the accumulation of sediment at the mouth of a river. (2) Income to a trust that comes from some unusual source. (3) The right of heirs and legatees to claim the property of any other heir or legatee who refuses to accept it or dies before inheriting.
An accounting method that records incurred expenses and income earned during a period even if all money has not been paid or received; businesses that maintain inventory must use this method.
(1) To accumulate or increase; to receive at regular intervals; to become due. (2) To come into existence as a cause of action. N. accrual.
Income earned but not yet received.
Interest due on the principal.
Earnings and profits from previous years that have not yet been distributed as dividends. earnings and profits,
Accusing someone of a crime.
A group such as a grand jury that hears evidence and decides that someone should be accused of a crime.
A document that sets forth an accusation.
The stage of criminal procedure after the accused has been arrested and is being interrogated.
To charge someone with a crime; to institute legal proceedings against a suspected criminal. N. accusation.
Someone charged with a crime.
To admit or confirm; to accept responsibility. N. acknowledgement.
American Civil Liberties Union.
To accept without protest; to give implied consent by silence. N. acquiescence. See also laches. acquiescence,
To gain or obtain; to become the owner of something.
The process of acquiring; something acquired.
To set free or release; to absolve of criminal liability. N. acquittal.
A unit of land with an area of 43,560 square feet (4,840 square yards).
Accelerated Cost Recovery System.
To do something,
Something that happens as a result of natural forces that cannot be controlled by humans, such as storms, earthquakes, or floods. Also called act of providence.
An official document certifying that a sale has occurred, signed by the parties and verified by witnesses and a notary.
Temporarily performing the duties of a position or office without officially holding that position or office.
(Latin) No action; a pleading of nonperformance; a nonsuit.

(Latin) (1) A proceeding or an action; the right to pursue a lawsuit. (2) A court proceeding; a lawsuit; a formal complaint brought by one party to prosecute another or demand rights within a court of law. See also case.
An action brought in a court of equity.
An action brought in a court of law.
Forming the legal basis of a cause of action.
A trust that must be actively managed by the trustee. trust,
Intentional and purposeful concealment.
The state of serving full-time in the military; see reserve.
Negligence that occurs through some positive act as opposed to passive inaction.
Someone who engages in some conduct that is part of the commission of a crime. activism,
A judge who uses his or her position on the bench to make decisions that reflect personal attitudes and beliefs instead of applying the letter of the law or following precedent.
A person who acts; one who performs an action.

Information expressly given to a person so that there is no question whether or not it has been received, or information that he or she should have had because there was sufficient evidence available.
Damages awarded for real injuries as opposed to nominal or punitive damages.
The place where someone actually lives, as opposed to a legal residence. See also constructive.
A value awarded in condemnation proceedings based on the price that a property would probably fetch from a willing buyer to a willing seller.

Statistical tables that predict the likely ages that people will reach; used for such purposes as calculating the value of annuities or damages stemming from premature deaths; also called life tables.
Someone who uses statistics to calculate insurance rates. ADJ. actuarial.
(Latin) The guilty act; a criminal action. See also mens rea.
(Latin) To the damage; the clause in a complaint in which a plaintiff specifies the damages he or she seeks.
(Latin) For this;
(Latin) To the person; appealing to the emotions instead of to logic and reason. ADV. ad hominem.
(Latin) To infinity; repeatedly; forever.
(Latin) For the lawsuit; for the purposes of the lawsuit being prosecuted. ad litem,
(Latin) According to the value; in proportion to value.
A tax assessed on the value of property.
Americans with Disabilities Act.
To join together; unite; attach.
Something that is added on; usually written material added to the end of a document.

Someone who uses, does, or consumes something habitually and who is dependent on that substance or activity; usually applies to those who are dependent on drugs or alcohol and cannot control their consumption thereof.
Something added to something else; a new structure added to an existing building.

(Latin) A trial court's increase in the amount of damages awarded to a plaintiff by a jury; this can be done with the defendant's consent if the court rules that the jury's award is inadequate, and on the condition that the plaintiff's motion for a new trial will be denied.
Age Discrimination in Employment Act.
To remove, revoke, or take away; to take away a legacy or future bequest in advance.
Revocation of a legacy by a testator before the testator's death, sometimes by giving the recipient the property
Sufficient, satisfactory.
Care appropriate to the risk in question.
Under eminent domain, the market value of property when taken.
fitness.
A remedy that provides complete and appropriate relief.
Joining; clinging to; sticking together;

A contract offered to one party by another on a take-it-or-leave-it basis, in which the offering party creates all the details of the contract and the receiving party has no opportunity to bargain or modify the contract. This is common with standard form contracts. Often there is doubt as to whether an adhesion contract is valid because one party has so little bargaining power. See also unconscionable.
Next to; near; neighboring.
Rules of procedure; the rules that administer substantive law.
Joined with; touching; in contact with.
To postpone; to suspend; to stop with the intent of resuming later.
To decide; to pass judgment; to sentence.
To judge; to formally issue a final judgment in a court proceeding.
To alter; to make satisfactory; to remove discrepancies; to settle.
A mortgage in which the interest rate is tied to an index and periodically moves up or down as the index does. ABBRV. ARM. mortgage,
Acquisition cost plus capital improvements minus depreciation. basis,
Basis plus additions to capital minus depreciation.
Gross income minus deductions. income,
Gross income minus expenses and capital loss deduction.
A person who settles things, especially insurance matters; one who determines the amount of a claim against an insurer and then agrees on a settlement with the insured.
To manage; to run (a business or other operation); to make someone take an oath; to enforce a decree.
The process of managing or running something; the people who manage something.
A governmental organization that implements a particular piece of legislation, such as workers' compensation or tax law.
The body of laws that governs administrative agencies.

The officer who presides over administrative hearings. This officer is not a judge of law; he or she may administer oaths, hear testimony, make determinations of fact, and recommend or make decisions.
A federal law that governs the proceedings of federal administrative agencies.
A person appointed by a court to handle the estate of someone who dies intestate, i.e., without a will. FEM. administratrix. See also executor.
Jurisdiction over events happening on the seas, oceans, and navigable waters.
A court that hears disputes related to admiralty and maritime matters.
Acceptable; valid; able to be admitted.
Evidence that is proper to admit at trial because it is relevant to the matter at hand. See also admit, evidence.
(1) To allow in; to accept as evidence (2) To acknowledge that something is true; to confess to a crime. N. admission.
(1) To make one's own; to accept; to choose. (2) To create a legal parent-child relationship between people unrelated by blood. N. adoption.
A fully grown person; one who has reached the age of majority.
Entertainment not suitable for children, typically erotic or sexual acts or movies; pornography.
To corrupt; to mix into a pure or good substance something of poorer quality. N. adulteration.
Voluntary sexual congress between a married person and someone who is not his or her spouse. ADJ. adulterous.

To move forward or make progress; to approach; to pay money or some other payment before it is due, as when an author receives an advance before a book is written. N. Money paid before it is due; money or other objects given on credit, with some future repayment anticipated.
A document such as a living will or durable power of attorney that describes the kind of medical care a person wishes to receive if he or she becomes incapacitated.
Recent judicial opinions published in pamphlet or loose-leaf form that are later compiled into bound volumes of regional reporters; hardbound collections of court opinions.
A portion of an anticipated inheritance paid before the death of the testator (usually a parent to a child) that is then deducted from the recipient's share of the estate after the testator's death.
A hazardous and risky enterprise or activity; often used in marine insurance policies as a synonym for "peril."
Opponent; opposing counsel.
A hearing or trial with opposing parties, one seeking relief from the other, and that ends with one party receiving a favorable outcome at the expense of the other. See also case.
Against; opposed; contrary; hostile.

A method of acquiring property without buying it; if a person uses land not belonging to him or her in a manner that is open (so that the owner knows about it or should know), but without permission of the owner, continuously, actually, and exclusively for a period of time prescribed by statute (usually a number of years), then a court will find that the person has earned title to the land.
To describe publicly in order to promote sales or solicit applications; to announce; to call to public attention.
A public notice promoting a product or service or soliciting applications.
The practice of creating advertisements.
Guidance; suggestions; recommendations intended to help someone; the opinions and recommendations given to clients by lawyers.
To counsel; to recommend a course of action; to offer helpful suggestions. ADJ. advisory.

An opinion rendered by a judge or court that indicates how the court would rule on a question without actually ruling on some adversary proceeding; an advisory opinion is only informative and is not binding.
Pleading or arguing for a cause.
Someone who supports a cause or argues for something; someone who defends another; a legal counselor or representative. V. To argue in support or defense of; to recommend.
seditious.
A computer program that automatically displays advertisements on a computer, often bundled with another program that users buy for actual use. See also bundling.
The value of something due to its beauty or artistic worth rather than its practical use.
To have an effect on; to influence; to change.
Someone who makes and swears to an affidavit.
A written statement of facts whose truth is confirmed by oath of the party making it, used as evidence in court.
To associate with; to join officially. N. A person or organization allied with or officially joined to a larger body.
The condition of being affiliated.
(1) A relationship; fondness or liking. (2) The degree of relationship between people who are related by marriage. See also consanguinity.
To confirm; state as fact; ratify; to declare that a previous judgment is correct.
The act of confirming that something is true. It can be used as a substitute for an oath in the case of religious or ethical objections to swearing.
An answer filed by a defendant that states the existence of facts that contradict the plaintiff's allegations, rather than simply denying those allegations. pleading,
Deliberate and positive efforts to help victims of discrimination by remedying effects of past discrimination and preventing future discrimination.

In pleading, a response to a complaint that constitutes a defense and justification for the defendant's actions instead of attacking the truth of the plaintiff's allegations; affirmative defenses include self-defense, assumption of risk, estoppel, and insanity.
A deliberate act done by someone who knows it is wrong; deliberately misrepresenting a fact or concealing a fact.
Relief or compensation granted to a defendant in a lawsuit.
To attach; fasten to something.
American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations.
Said earlier; previously mentioned.

Premeditated; planned in advance. a fortiori. ADJ. (Latin) With a stronger reason; used in argument to describe a proposition that must be true because it is a subcategory of something that is true. ADV. a fortiori.
An examination of a dead body to determine cause of death.
Subsequent to a particular event. See also accessory.
Acquired subsequent to a particular time or event.
(1) Property a debtor acquires after concluding an agreement putting up other property as security for a loan. (2) Property acquired by a bankrupt after filing for bankruptcy.
A doctrine providing that if someone sells property he or she does not own but then acquires the title to it, the buyer receives the good title.
To oppose; to rebut.
Describes a statement or admission that is disadvantageous to the person making it.

term: against the (manifest) weight of the evidence
definition: Not supported by the evidence presented at trial, or based on false evidence; the evidence clearly leading to the opposite conclusion.
Illegal; also describes a court decision made despite insufficient evidence or material issues.
Describes the state of mind of a victim of robbery or rape.
Unfair treatment in the workplace and in hiring based on age; prohibited by the federal Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967.
A federal law that prohibits discrimination against people on the basis of age. ABBRV. ADEA.

(1) The age at which a person may marry without his or her parents' permission. (2) The age at which a person may consent to have sexual intercourse without causing his or her sexual partner to be guilty of an offense such as statutory rape or sexual assault.
The age at which a person becomes a legal adult, responsible for his or her own actions, and able to enter into contracts and vote.
The age at which a child is deemed responsible for his or her own actions, usually 7 years of age.

(1) A relationship in which one person, the agent, is authorized to act on behalf of the other, the principal. (2) A department or group that performs a specific task for the government. (3) A business that provides a specific service, often arranging transactions between customers.
A person authorized to act for another person, the principal, in specific or unlimited ways. agent,
To make worse. N. aggravation. See also simple.
Assault made worse than simple assault by the addition of aggravating circumstances, such as extreme indifference to human life or the use of a deadly weapon.
Circumstances that increase the severity of a crime or tort.

The sum of several parts; the entire amount of something. V. To combine several elements into one whole, as when several causes of action are joined into a single lawsuit or several people are combined to form a group for a class action. N. aggregation.
Feeling anger at some injury or unfair treatment.
Someone who has been injured, suffered a loss, or whose rights or property are at risk.
Arranging accounts in chronological order of days outstanding.
Having to do with land and agriculture.
To concur; to consent; to settle.
A mutual understanding between two or more parties; a meeting of minds. It often leads to a contract.
To knowingly help someone commit a crime. See also accessory, accomplice.
To protest; to express disapproval; in court, to protest that the opposing party's action or statement is improper or illegal.

Rights to use the air above land; air rights accompany land ownership of land, with some limitations, e.g., a landowner has the right to recover damages from airlines that interfere with his or her use of the land, but is not allowed to pollute the air.
Also known as. See also alias.
A city or town official or legislator; a councilman.
Random, uncertain, depending on chance.
A contract whose performance depends on some random event, such as an insurance contract.
A fake or alternate identity. ADV. Used to indicate the use of a false name, e.g., "Samuel Clemens, alias Mark Twain."
A defense in which the defendant claims to have been someplace other than the scene of a crime when the crime was
A foreigner; someone born in another country who has not become a citizen of his or her country of residence. ADJ. (1) Foreign. (2) Strange, unfamiliar. See also resident alien.
Able to be transferred.
To transfer property from one person to another. N. alienation.
The tort of maliciously interfering with a marriage, resulting in damage to the marital relationship.
An allowance ordered by the court that one spouse pays to the other after a divorce or separation for the support and maintenance of the recipient.
To claim; to assert; to state in a pleading what one intends to prove at trial. N. allegation.

A charge by the court to a deadlocked jury admonishing them to work together to reach a verdict, and especially encouraging the holders of a minority opinion to listen to the arguments of the majority and to let themselves be convinced if possible. This is prohibited in some states.
To distribute for a particular purpose; to assign; to allot. In taxation, to allot portions of income to different purposes. N.
allocable.
A speech by a trial judge to a criminal defendant asking the defendant if he or she would like to make a statement on his or her own behalf, or asking if the defendant has any legal
To apportion; to distribute portions of something to several individuals; to allocate something to someone.
The amount of something allocated to someone; a portion or share.
To permit; to acquiesce; to accept as true.
(1) The amount that is permitted. (2) A sum of money paid to someone regularly.
Land formed by the accumulation of silt on the banks or at the mouth of a stream or river. See also accrete. also known as. ADJ. See alias.
To change or modify. N. alteration. alteration,
(Latin) Other self; an alternative personality. In corporate law, a doctrine that allows the court to find an individual responsible for acts done in the name of a corporation.
A bequest in which the testator leaves a recipient one of several things without specifying which. legacy,
Methods for settling disputes without recourse to litigation, including arbitration, mediation, and conciliation.
A form of pleading in which the pleader sets forth two or more different and possibly inconsistent versions of his or her claim or defense.
Uncertainty of meaning;
A derogatory term for a lawyer who represents plaintiffs in personal injury cases.
To fix; to improve; to modify or revise a document. N. amendment.
A pleading that either corrects or expands an earlier pleading. pleading,
Reparations; something done to make up for a wrong done to someone else.
A national association of lawyers and law students. ABBRV. ABA.
A national organization dedicated to protecting constitutionally guaranteed civil rights and liberties. ABBRV. ACLU.

A digest of U.S. cases arranged chronologically and by subject, serving as a guide to American case law. American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations. N. U.S. trade union organization made up of over fifty national and international trade unions representing over ten million workers. ABBRV. AFL-CIO.
A federal statute prohibiting discrimination against disabled people in jobs, transportation, and services. ABBRV. ADA.

(Latin) Friend of the court; someone who is not a party to a lawsuit but who has a strong interest in the subject matter of a case and petitions the court for permission to file a brief providing information on the matter to aid the court in rendering its decision; such a brief is called an amicus curiae brief or amicus brief.
An official pardon granted by a government to a group of people forgiving them for past crimes, usually political crimes such as treason or draft evasion.

To spread out the payment of a debt by periodically paying a portion of interest and capital; to pay off a debt such as a mortgage in installments; to write off the cost of an asset over time. N. amortization.
A person who wishes to bring about a state of anarchy.

A political system characterized by an absence of government and law-enforcement mechanisms and complete freedom for individuals; a state of lawlessness or disorder resulting from a lack of governing authority.
A person from whom one is descended.
A line of family descent.
Old; having existed since the distant past.
In evidence law, old (twenty or thirty years old) documents kept in official custody, presumed to be genuine without express proof.
Supplementary; additional; supporting.

Jurisdiction claimed by a federal district court to adjudicate matters raised as part of an existing controversy, even though the court would not ordinarily have jurisdiction over the matter. See also supplemental jurisdiction.
A suit that grows out of an existing or prior suit in the same court.
(Latin) Intentionally. animo,
(Latin) Intending to make a will.
(Latin) Will, intent.
(Latin) Intent to give.
To add something to something else; to join; to unite. N. An addition to a building or document.
The act of adding or joining one thing to another.
To add notes to a text explaining it or commenting on it;
annotated.

A yearly financial report prepared by a corporation for its stockholders, audited by an independent certified public accountant, and filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K, including a balance sheet, income statement, and other information.
One who receives an annuity.
A fixed sum paid to a person periodically for a set period of time, often for life.
(1) To declare something invalid; to abolish; to make nonexistent. (2) To declare that a marriage was never legally valid and therefore never existed. N. annulment. See also divorce.

(1) A response; a reaction. (2) A pleading submitted by a defendant in response to a plaintiff's complaint, containing denials of allegations, affirmative defenses, and counterclaims. V. To respond; to file an answer to a complaint with the court.
Before marriage.

An agreement entered into by prospective spouses before their marriage, to take effect upon marriage. It usually contains provisions about support and distribution of property in the event of divorce or separation. See also prenuptial agreement.
A defensive missile designed to meet and destroy a ballistic missile, i.e., a missile carrying some nuclear, chemical, biological, or other agent intended to inflict harm on its victims. ABBRV. ABM.

A law that provides for the heirs of a devisee to inherit the devisee's share of an inheritance if the devisee dies before the testator, instead of allowing the bequest to lapse as it would have under common law; e.g., if a mother dies before her father, her children can inherit her share of their grandfather's estate.
(1) To do something before its proper time. (2) To expect, to predict. N.
anticipatory.
A breach of contract that occurs when one party announces before the time scheduled for performance that he or she will not perform according to the contract.
A crime whose purpose is to commit a crime, such as conspiracy.
A federal law enacted in 1999 that makes cybersquatting a wrongful act subject to civil liability.
Intended to prevent monopolies and trusts and to promote competition in business. See also Sherman Antitrust Act, Clayton Act.
Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation. a posteriori. ADJ. (Latin) Relating to knowledge gained through recent observation or experience. See also a priori.
Obvious; evident; true as far as one can see.
The authority an agent gains when a principal allows a third party to believe that the agent has such authority, whether or not it is part of the agent's actual authority.
A defect that can readily be seen on inspection.
To request a higher court to review a case that has been decided by a lower court and render a new decision,
A court that can hear appeals; also called a court of appeals or an appellate court.
To come into court as a party to a lawsuit and submit to the court's jurisdiction. N. appearance. appearance,
One who files an appeal.
Having to do with appeals.
A court that reviews decisions made by lower courts or administrative agencies and does not hear new cases.
The party against whom an appeal is filed; the party who prevailed at trial in the lower court. See also respondent.
A computer program; software that performs a particular task, such as a word processor, a spreadsheet, or a Web browser.
To select, choose, or designate; to assign someone a job.
One who has been appointed.
(1) The designation of a person to do a job. (2) A person appointed to a position. See also power of appointment.
(1) To divide something and assign or allocate portions of it to different parties. (2) To distribute legislative seats among the parties to be represented. N. apportionment.
To assess the value of something; to evaluate. N. appraisal.
(1) To grow in value. (2) To understand; to realize fully the implications of something.
The increase in an item's market value over a period of time; the difference between an item's cost basis and fair market value.
(1) To take something for one's own; often used to describe theft. (2) To allocate money or other items for a particular purpose. N. appropriation.
Something attached or appended to something else.
An easement that is attached to a particular piece of property (the dominant estate or benefited property) and stays with it if it is transferred to another owner. easement,
A referee; someone appointed by a court to settle a dispute by the rules of law or equity. See also arbitrator.
A financial transaction in which securities or goods are simultaneously bought in one market and sold in another; price differences in the different markets produce a profit.
A name for a product that is in no way descriptive or suggestive of the product, such as "Apple" computers, that is easily registered but difficult to educate consumers about. trademark,
Describes a decision made according to whim and without regard to facts or law.
A form of dispute resolution in which a neutral third party renders a decision after both parties speak for themselves at a hearing. See also arbitrator.
A clause in a contract that requires disputes under the contract to be submitted to arbitration; such clauses are designed to avoid the litigation of disputes.

A neutral person appointed or chosen to settle a dispute by hearing arguments from both parties and then rendering a decision at his or her own discretion, not bound by rules of law or equity. See also arbiter.
A place where old records, articles, documents, or books are kept; a place on a website or computer where old articles are stored and can be accessed.
