(Latin) Between the living; describes gifts or transfers done from one living person to another. ADV. inter vivos.

To catch or obstruct something on its way to a destination; to use electronic or other devices to obtain the contents of a communication done through some telecommunications device, such as a wiretap. N. interception.
The percentage of interest that a lender charges as a fee for lending money. rate,
The percentage used to calculate interest due on a loan.
A person with a legally recognized private interest in some matter.
In the meantime; during intervening time; temporary.
A short-term loan held until long-term or permanent financing can be arranged for an enterprise; also called a bridge loan.
A temporary order issued to handle a matter until a specified event happens or until a final order is issued. See also interlocutory.

People who serve on the boards of directors of two or more corporations simultaneously, resulting in a situation called an interlocking directorate, which can be used to restrict competition and is often illegal under antitrust law.
A temporary sentence imposed on a defendant between conviction and final sentencing. sentence,
An order issued by the court while a trial is still in process, determining an intermediate issue before coming to a final decision for the entire case.
A computer network restricted to a given workplace or facility, not accessible by the general public.
A federal statute in Title 26 of the U.S. Code setting forth laws on federal taxes, including income tax, estate tax, gift tax, excise tax,
The federal agency that administers federal tax laws, assesses and collects taxes, audits taxpayers, and handles appeals by taxpayers who dispute their tax liability. ABBRV. IRS.
An agreement between two or more nations that creates legal rights or obligations among them; a treaty, convention, or other such agreement between nations.

The United Nations' court, seated at The Hague, Netherlands, that provides advice on questions of law and the writing of treaties and settles legal disputes between nations; its decisions are enforceable by the U.N. Security Council.
The body of law that governs relations between different countries, composed of custom and practice, rules and statutes, international treaties, and other sources.
Trade in goods or services across national borders.
A global network of computers, networks, and websites that allows the exchange of information through domain names, IP addresses, email, file sharing, and other mechanisms. See also World Wide Web.

term: Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers
definition: A nonprofit corporation in California that manages the assignment of IP addresses and domain names for the U.S.
(1) The set of rules governing communication among computers across a computer network or the Internet. (2) Intellectual property. ABBRV. IP.
A company that sells consumers access to the Internet. ABBRV. ISP.
An action in equity in which two or more parties who each claim property held by a third party submit the claim to a court to settle their rights, brought by the third party who fears
To insert words into a document,

To ascertain or explain the meaning of something, such as a statute, contract, or other document; to explain the meaning of the language of a law or other legal document without venturing into legal intentions and consequences. N. interpretation. See also construe, construction.
To question; to ask questions of someone such as a suspected criminal,
During discovery before trial, written questions about the case presented by one party to the opposing party that must be answered under oath and returned to the questioning party.
Existing in more than one state; carried on in or between two or more states.
Trade, transportation of people or property, and all forms of commerce that involve the territory of more than one state in the United States.

A federal commission that regulates surface transportation between states, including trucks, trains, freight forwarders, oil pipelines, and other commercial carriers, in order to facilitate interstate commerce by ensuring that the public will have access to transportation that is reliable and reasonably priced. ABBRV. ICC.
An agreement between two or more states on a matter of interest to each of them.
To come between two things in such a way as to delay, obstruct, or change the course of events. N. intervention.
Something that happens between the action that sets in motion a series of events and the ultimate result. cause,

An independent event that occurs between a wrongful act and an injury and changes the expected course of events so that the result is not the one that would have been predicted, thus relieving the person who committed the original wrongful act of liability for the injury. See also superseding cause.
A person who enters a lawsuit by intervention.
A procedure in which someone who is not originally a party to a lawsuit enters it to defend his or her own interest in the matter.
The condition of dying without a valid will.
Without a valid will at the time of death. N. A person who dies without a valid will.

Disposition of the property of someone who dies intestate according to the rules of descent and distribution. in the course of employment. ADJ. Occurring while an employee is at work or otherwise in his or her employer's service. in toto. ADV. (Latin) Totally; completely; as a whole.
An intoxicating substance.
To make drunk; to cause someone to lose control of his or her physical and mental faculties.
Drunk; under the influence of alcohol.

Drunkenness; a state of losing control of one's physical or mental faculties due to the consumption of alcohol or another intoxicating substance, which renders one incapable of acting like a reasonable person of ordinary prudence would under the same circumstances.
Within the walls; within the confines or boundaries of an organization or institution. See also extramural.
An internal network; a computer network restricted to a secure area and specific users.
Inherent; naturally part of something.
Evidence produced by questioning a witness at trial.
Fraud that occurs during trial through perjury, forged documents, or hiding or misrepresenting evidence, which affects the outcome of the trial.
The inherent value of a thing, which remains constant regardless of place, time, or special features that affect its market value.
(1) To take effect; to benefit someone; to vest. (2) To accustom someone to something.
Not valid; not legally adequate; void.
The wrongful and unwarranted intrusion into or publicizing of someone's private affairs by another person or the government.
To design or create some new thing that did not exist before; to create a story, often intending to deceive.
The process of creating some new thing, especially a device or machine; something invented.
A person who invents something.
A detailed list of property, such as goods in stock or the complete contents of an estate. See also first-in, first-out, lastin, first-out.

(1) To spend money on an enterprise in the hope that it will result in a profit; to give time or energy to a cause in the hope that it will generate good results. See also investment. (2) To bestow on someone an office or a right. (3) In feudal times, to bestow a grant of land on someone in a ceremony called investiture.
A company whose shares are bought by an investor.
To examine the facts surrounding an incident as part of a formal inquiry into the matter. N.
investigatory.
Powers given to governmental agencies to investigate violations of laws and the possible effects of proposed laws.
The expenditure of money on an enterprise in the hope of making a profit; assets or securities bought in the hope of earning a profit.
A financial institution or broker that buys newly issued securities and sells them to investors.
A company primarily in the business of trading securities, using its capital to invest in other companies.
Someone who buys shares in a company or otherwise invests money.
A person who enters another's property after being invited.
A list of goods contained in a shipment along with the price due for them; a bill. V. To send an invoice to someone for goods or services provided.
Accidental or unintentional killing of a person through negligence or recklessness.
A confession made unwillingly and without free choice by someone whose rights against self-incrimination have been violated by threat of violence, coercion, improper promises, etc.
The unintentional loss of property through theft, destruction, seizure, or condemnation.
Slavery; forcing someone to work for someone else.
The Greek letter "i," i.e., the smallest letter of the Greek alphabet; a miniscule amount, the smallest possible amount of something.
Internet Protocol.
Internet Protocol address; a computer address; the unique set of digits that computers use to identify a specific computer or device in a network.
Initial public offering.
(Latin) By the very fact itself; through the effect of a fact or act.
Individual retirement account.
Internal Revenue Code.
Not applicable to the matter at hand;
Impossible to repair.
An injury for which no adequate legal remedy exists, usually addressed by an injunction.
Unable to be withdrawn or revoked.
Internal Revenue Service.
Internet service provider.

(1) A problem or question; a topic for debate. (2) The act of sending something out formally; promulgation. (3) Children; descendants. (4) The act of offering corporate securities for sale. V. To send out or distribute; to promulgate; to offer something up for sale.
A disagreement about facts in a legal controversy, i.e., when one party suggests one version of facts and the other disagrees. IP address 264

term: it is called a legal separation
definition: separation.
Expenses individually listed and deducted from gross income. deduction,
"Judge" or "justice"; used as a title for a person holding that office.
Judge Advocate General.
A building used to confine people held in legal custody either awaiting trial for a criminal offense or serving a prison sentence after conviction;
An inmate in a correctional institution who has no formal legal training but has studied the law independently, who helps fellow inmates prepare appeals and provides legal advice to them.
Juris doctor.
A law requiring the government to provide a criminal defendant in a federal case with copies of witness statements made against him or her in order to prepare a defense.
Risk or danger; the danger of being convicted that is a natural accompaniment to being a defendant in a criminal trial. See also double jeopardy.
Laws used in the first half of the twentieth century to segregate the races. See also segregate, desegregate.
(1) A position of employment. (2) A task, usually paid. (3) A crime such as robbery. V. To buy and sell goods or stocks.
(1) A person who buys and sells stocks or goods; a wholesaler or middleman. (2) A person who works casually doing odd jobs for pay.
A fictitious name given to a party to a lawsuit who wishes to remain anonymous or whose name is unknown; a fictitious name given to a hypothetical character for purposes of argument.
To combine or unite; to unite with another party in a lawsuit; to add a cause of action or issue to a lawsuit. See also joinder.
The act of uniting parties or causes of action to a lawsuit. joinder,
Combining two or more causes of action into a single lawsuit.
The addition of one or more parties to a lawsuit.
A will disposing of joint property or property that is owned individually but is treated as common property, signed by all parties who hold the property. will,
Both shared and individual.

Liability for an enterprise shared by two or more people that can be applied to the group as a whole or to individuals, so that each is individually responsible for performing the entire obligation and each can be sued independently for failure to fulfill it, while at the same time the group is also responsible for the obligation and can be sued as a whole.
A bank account shared by two or more people who each have equal right to it.
An enterprise undertaken by two or more people who agree on a common purpose and share control of the project and a financial interest in it.
Liability owed by two or more people together.
A company that raises capital by pooling the contributions of its members, which are then divided into shares that are distributed to members to represent their share of ownership of the company.

A commercial enterprise similar to a partnership, in which two or more parties join together for the purpose of carrying out a single commercial enterprise, for which they share assets, liability, and control.
Done together.
(1) A daily record of transactions, business, proceedings, appointments, events and occurrences, etc. (2) A periodical publication covering a particular profession or field.
A privilege that protects journalists and other media professionals from being forced to reveal confidential sources of information in some cases.
Taking an automobile without the owner's permission in order to use it temporarily but not intending to keep it permanently.
Justice of the Peace.
An officer who presides over and decides cases in a court. V. To decide a legal case; to form an opinion about a matter. ABBRV. J.
A military lawyer or legal officer; an officer in the Judge Advocate General's corps.
A title used by military senior legal officers. ABBRV. JAG.
Case law created by judges who interpret statutes in what some consider to be an extreme or ideologically motivated way.
A lien that a judgment creditor is entitled to file against a judgment debtor's property, often in order to sell the property at a sheriff's sale. lien,
A judgment by the court for one party after receiving a verdict for the other, following a motion for a directed verdict; also called judgment n.o.v., non obstante veredicto.
A judgment rendered after the parties have presented their cases and the court has determined which party is in the right on the basis of facts and evidence presented.
Describes a person not worth suing for monetary damages because he or she is insolvent, has no property within a jurisdiction, or is otherwise protected from a judgment for money recoveries.
A court-ordered sale of property administered by an officer of the court. sale,

A form of legal action in which a judge writes opinions and renders decisions based on progressive ideas of social justice instead of basing his or her decisions strictly on judicial precedent and a restrained interpretation of legislation.
The power that comes as part of the office of judge; the power to hear cases and render decisions; also called judicial power.
The branch of government consisting of courts and other entities that exist to interpret and enforce laws.
A judge's immunity from civil liability for anything done in his or her official capacity.

A practice in which the court will recognize certain facts without requiring one of the parties to produce evidence proving them, usually in the case of facts that are universally acknowledged to be true or facts that the judge or jury already know.

(1) The power of the courts to review the acts of other branches of the government, usually in order to determine that the law is properly applied to a matter, especially in constitutional matters; review by the Supreme Court of acts by the legislature, as established in the case Marbury v. Madison. (2) Review of a trial court's decision by an appellate court.
A collective term for judges; the bench; the judicial branch of the government.
For a criminal defendant to leave the jurisdiction or fail to appear at trial after bail has been posted for him or her, causing whoever posted bail to forfeit it. See also bail, bondsman.
Relating to the law, justice, rights, or obligations.
Having to do with legal proceedings, judges, or the administration of justice.
(Latin) Of law.
The degree received by someone who completes law school. ABBRV. JD.

The power to make judicial decisions; a court's or judge's power to investigate the facts of a matter, apply law to them, and declare a judgment. (2) The territory in which a particular court can exercise its authority; the system of courts within a particular area. ADJ. jurisdictional.
The amount of money required to be involved in a controversy in order to give a court jurisdiction over it.
(1) The philosophy or science of law. (2) The body of law formed by cases and interpretations of them; a system of law.
A scholar of the law; a lawyer or judge.
A member of a jury.

A group of people selected and sworn to hear the evidence in a case and decide what the true facts are; usually composed of a cross section of the community. See also grand jury, hung jury, petit jury. jury,
A party's rejection of a potential juror while selecting a jury for trial.

A statement made to the jurors by the judge at the conclusion of testimony but before the jurors begin deliberating, in which the judge describes the law that applies to the matters in controversy and explains how the jury should apply it.
The group of people summoned to court on a particular day to serve as jurors if chosen.
The process of choosing members of a jury from a panel of potential jurors, done by the parties to a lawsuit at the start of trial.

A trial that takes place before a jury, in which the jury hears the testimony and sees evidence and then renders a decision about the facts in accordance with the judge's instructions. See also bench trial.

(Latin) Compelling law; a peremptory norm; in international law, a principle that is widely accepted to be true by a large number of states and individuals, such as the belief that genocide and slavery are wrong.
(Latin) Law in war; law governing actions during wartime.
Fair; lawful; morally right.
A legitimate or fair reason for doing something; a reason that will legally justify an action.
Compensation given to an owner whose property is taken through eminent domain that is fair both to the owner, who is losing property, and to the public, who is paying for it.
A power or privilege; authority to do something; a legal, equitable, or moral entitlement to something; an individual liberty. See also bill of rights, civil rights, privacy.
(1) Fairness; fair and just administration of law; fair treatment or behavior. (2) A judge; a magistrate; a judge sitting on the Supreme Court of a state or country.
The federal department that enforces federal laws and represents the federal government in legal matters, under the direction of the attorney general.

A magistrate with minor local authority, who hears minor criminal and civil cases, performs marriages, and handles other civil matters; most states have replaced justices of the peace with other courts and officers. ABBRV. J.P.
Able to be tried in court; presenting real interests instead of merely hypothetical or abstract ones.
A controversy involving a real issue that can be settled by a court, involving a present claim made by one party and another party disputing it.
Excusable; defensible; able to be proven to be reasonable or correct. See also homicide, justifiable.
A child; a person below the age of legal majority. ADJ. Childish or youthful; relating to children or young people.
A court that hears cases involving children and teenagers, particularly over juvenile delinquents and neglected children.
A minor who commits a crime or engages in regular criminal activity. See also delinquent child.

A court that is completely biased against one party and therefore disregards that party's rights and delivers a judgment or verdict that is unfair to him or her; a trial in a kangaroo court is considered a sham without legal authority.
A retirement plan for self-employed people with benefits similar to those enjoyed by employees with employersponsored retirement plans.
A system of organizing published cases by numbering each point of law with a key number, making it easy for legal researchers to locate relevant topics and cases.
A payment made by a seller to a buyer or agent in order to persuade him or her to enter into the transaction; illegal in most cases.
To unlawfully and forcibly take and carry away a person. N. kidnapping.
The abduction and holding of a person in order to demand money in return for the victim's release. See also abduct, false arrest, ransom.

To write fraudulent checks; to write checks on an account with insufficient funds in the hopes that funds sufficient to cover the check will be deposited before the check is presented to the bank for payment. N. kiting, check kiting. See also float.
To be aware of; to have information about something; to understand.
With awareness; consciously; with understanding of the circumstances and the implications of an act.
(1) Work, especially manual work. (2) A collective term for workers. V. To work, especially at hard physical jobs.
A disagreement between employees and their employer about terms and conditions of employment, including pay, safety, hours, etc.
The relationship between employers and employees, usually as personified by management and employees.

An organization of workers in a particular field that exists to handle disputes between the workers and their employers, working under the assumption that the workers have more power when they join forces and negotiate as a unit; also called a labor organization.
A person who engages in physical work for wages.
An unreasonable delay in asserting or enforcing a claim. laches,
ignorant.
(French) Allow to do; an economic doctrine stating that the free market should be allowed to operate free of governmental interference.
A public official still in office after the election but before the inauguration of his or her successor.

(1) Soil or earth, including plants growing on it and rocks embedded in it. (2) Property, especially in the form of land; real property. land, tenements, and hereditaments. N. A legal term encompassing all types of real property.
An explosive device laid on the surface of the earth or hidden just underneath it, intended to explode when a person or vehicle touches it.

A form of trust in which a trustee holds legal title to a piece of property but the beneficiary has the power to direct the trustee, manage the property, and receive income from it; also called an Illinois land trust.
Consisting of land; owning land.
A person who owns real property and rents it to tenants. See also lessor.

(1) A feature of the landscape or an object placed on it that marks the boundary of a piece of property. (2) A building or natural object that is easy to spot, or that has some historical or cultural significance. (3) An important event that marks a turning point or a significant moment.
A decision by the Supreme Court that changes the established law in an area.

The expiration of a right or privilege due to failure to exercise it or otherwise maintain it; the end of a legacy or right due to the death of the person who held it or to the expiration of a specified period of time; failure of a bequest or testamentary gift; the termination of an insurance policy if the policyholder does not pay the premium. V. lapse.
Taking the property of another person without the owner's consent and with the intention of making it the property of someone else. larceny,
Overtly sexual; lewd.
A doctrine that allows a plaintiff to recover from a defendant if the defendant had the last clear chance or opportunity to avoid the accident, regardless of the plaintiff's own negligence. last-in,

A will; the most recent will created by a deceased person; traditionally, the word "will" referred to the disposition of real property and the word "testament" to the disposition of personal property.
usage_examples: referred to the disposition of real property and the word

definition: Hidden; existing but not yet obvious or apparent.
Language that appears to be clear and understandable but that in fact presents ambiguities or confusion when someone tries to apply it.
A defect in an object that is not apparent on reasonable inspection or through the use of ordinary care.

term: Latin) Arising out of a tort
definition: ex delicto.

(1) A system of rules created by a society to regulate behavior and punish crimes. (2) A statute. (3) The professional field concerned with the rules that regulate society. law, at. ADJ. Pertaining to law; related to the law or the legal profession.
Seeing that people follow laws and catching and punishing lawbreakers; the work done by police.

The doctrine that if a court decides an issue of law, the determination will stay the same throughout subsequent trials of the same facts unless a higher court reconsiders the issue and issues a new determination.

The established law of a society, especially the principles of due process and other rights too fundamental to be abolished by government; in the United States, the Constitution and laws made by its authority.

United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea; an international agreement that sets rules for the use of the world's oceans, establishing territorial boundaries and governing the management of marine resources.
A professional journal published by a law school containing scholarly articles on legal topics by experts and
A test that is required for admission to most law schools. ABBRV. LSAT. laws of war. The laws that govern the actions nations may take when they are at war. See also Geneva Conventions, jus in bello.
legally.
Not conforming to law; not controlled by law.
An action at law or equity; a dispute brought before a court for determination.
An attorney; a person who has studied law or who practices law.
Not professional or expert in a particular field; nonecclesiastical or nonclergy.
A judge who has not studied law.
A witness who is not an expert in the field on which he or she is called to testify; see also expert.
A person who is not a professional or expert in a particular field, e.g., in legal situations, a nonlawyer.
The firing of one or many employees in response to business conditions, not for any wrongdoing of the employee; the temporary or permanent termination of one or more workers. See also downsize.
The head lawyer on one side of a lawsuit who is in charge of managing the case and all other attorneys and other people working on it.
A case that is recognized as determining the law on a particular topic and is often cited for that purpose.
A question in which the questioner hints or suggests to the witness the answer he or she would like to receive; not allowed during direct examination.

A contract in which the owner of a piece of property allows someone else to possess it exclusively for a specified period of time in return for payment. V. To allow someone to use property for a period of time in exchange for payment; to rent. See also let, lessor, lessee, sublease. lease,
An estate in real property held by lease, usually of a fixed duration; see also freehold.
Permission.
A period of time in which a person has permission to be absent from work or duties, after which he or she intends to return.
The court's permission to do a particular act.
A bequest; money or property left to someone by a will. legacy,
Valid money; money that a creditor must accept as payment of a debt.
The age at which a person can enter into contracts and conduct business on his or her own behalf, or the age at which a person can marry; majority.
A nonprofit system that provides legal assistance to people who cannot afford to pay for lawyers themselves.
The right to bring a lawsuit; the condition of being of sound mind, being of legal age, and suffering from no legal restraints or other impediments to bringing a lawsuit.
The conduct or event that directly causes an injury; proximate cause.
Injury or disadvantage caused to someone who acted in such a way as to change his or her legal position or assume a liability or duty based on a promise that another person made but did not keep.
An obligation required by law, such as the duty of parents to care for their children or the duty arising from a contract; also called a legal obligation.
An assumption that certain facts are true that is made by the court in order to render a legal decision without delay.

A person's official name registered with the government, usually consisting of one or more given names recorded on the person's birth certificate, the parents' or father's family name, and possibly a new family name acquired upon marriage.
A secretary with special expertise in legal matters.
A corporation established by Congress in 1974 that provides funding for legal assistance in noncriminal proceedings for people who cannot afford to pay for legal counsel. See also legal aid.
Money that can be used to pay for things; all coins and currencies of the United States, regardless of when they were issued.
The formal language used in legal documents; especially refers derogatorily to unnecessarily complex and opaque language that is extremely difficult to understand.
A person who receives a legacy.
To create or pass a law or resolution.
The act of making laws; laws created by a legislature.
Holding the power to create laws;
A member of a body that creates laws.
The group of people who make laws for a state or country.

(Latin) Master of laws; a post-graduate degree offered by law schools to students who have done advanced study in a legal specialty or to foreign lawyers who have taken a certain number of classes at an American law school. ABBRV. LL.M.
A defective or unsatisfactory automobile.
Laws passed by many states that provide relief to people who purchase new automobiles that turn out to be defective.

(1) To let someone use something temporarily, on the understanding that it will be returned; to allow someone to borrow something. (2) To provide someone with money that he or she must eventually pay back, usually with interest.
A person or organization that lends something, particularly money.
The act or business of lending something, especially money. See also loan.

term: Lending money at excessively high interest rates
definition: usurious.
A person who holds property through a lease; a person who rents property; a tenant. See also lease, lessor.

A crime that is inherently part of a more serious crime; e.g., assault is an inherent part of the more serious crime of assault and battery, or attempt is an inherent part of any crime that is actually completed.
A person who rents or leases property to someone else; a landlord.
(1) To rent; to allow someone to use property in exchange for payment; to grant a license to use property. (2) To award a contract for a project to one of several applicants who have submitted bids.
A promise made by a bank or other financial institution to another bank or financial institution that it will honor demands for payment on behalf of a specified customer under specified conditions.
A document that records the intention of the parties to it to enter into a contract or take some other action but that is not itself a formal contract.
A strict, literal, word-for-word interpretation of laws and statutes. See also spirit of the law.
(1) The use of borrowed money to try to earn much more through investment, in the hopes that the profits will outweigh the interest owed on the debt. (2) The ratio of a business's debt to equity.

A method by which outside investors or a corporation's own management buy the outstanding stock of a corporation using money borrowed from outside sources, and using the purchased company's assets as security for the loan.

(1) To impose a tax, fee, or fine on someone; to raise or collect. (2) To seize money or property. (3) To begin to wage war. N. (1) A tax or other sum collected from people. (2) Raising money through seizing property and selling it. (3) The enlisting of troops; a group of enlisted soldiers.
Sexually indecent; provoking lust. See also lascivious, indecent.
The crime of a man and woman living together without being married.
Sexual indecency, especially performed in public or in the presence of people who might be offended by it.

A privately owned online legal research system that provides access to reported decisions, federal and state statutes, law review articles, and many other materials; also called Lexis. See also Westlaw.

The condition of being responsible for something; something for which someone is responsible; an obligation or duty to do something or pay a sum of money; a possible claim against a person or business.
Insurance that covers an individual against claims against him or her made by third parties.
responsibility.
A written, printed, or published false and malicious statement that injures someone's reputation; the written form of defamation. ADJ. libelous. See also defamation, slander.
Open to new opinions and interpretations; not strict, conservative, or narrow-minded; in favor of individual liberty; generous. See also construction.
To remove restrictions.
Specific instances of freedom from restrictions; rights or privileges. See also civil liberties.
Freedom; freedom from restrictions except for those imposed by law to prevent people from interfering with the liberty of others.

(1) A permit that allows someone to do some act; permission to do something that would be illegal without that permission. (2) Permission to use property owned by someone else, which does not give the licensee an exclusive right of possession; see also lease.
A fee imposed by the government for granting a license or a privilege to do something; also called a license tax.
A person who receives a license; a person who has the right to use property or do some action through a license.
A person who grants a license.
(1) To intentionally make a false statement; to tell an untruth. (2) For an action or claim to be sustainable or admissible. N. A falsehood or untruth told intentionally to mislead someone.
A claim placed on property to act as security for a debt owed by the owner, held until the owner pays the debt and kept if the owner never pays. lien,
A person who holds an interest in property until the end of a life, either the tenant's or someone else's. tenants,
An estate in property that lasts until the person holding it dies, or until some other specified person dies.
The number of years a person is expected to live, based on age and sex and calculated by actuarial tables and statistics. life insurance. See insurance.
A claim to or interest in property that ends at the death of the person holding the interest or of some other person; see also life estate, life tenant.
A person who holds a tenancy on land that ends at his or her death or at the death of another person; such a tenancy is called a life tenancy. See also life estate.
Last-in, first-out.
An exchange of an item of property for another item of property of a similar type and value, usually nontaxable.
To restrict; to set boundaries. N. A boundary or restriction.

A restriction; a restriction of the time in which a lawsuit can be filed; a restriction of who can receive an estate in property or the uses to which an estate may be restricted. See also statute of limitations.
A partner whose liability for partnership debts is limited to his or her share of contributed capital, and who has only a limited share of profits or losses. partner,
Jurisdiction over only specific kinds of cases or otherwise restricted by statute; see also special jurisdiction.
Liability for corporate losses that is restricted in some way, usually to the amount that an investor or shareholder has placed in the corporation and not reaching personal assets.
A business organization, often managed by its members, with limited liability and limited ability to transfer ownership. ABBRV. L.L.C.

A form of partnership in which one or more general partners manage the business and are personally responsible for its debts, and one or more limited partners contribute money and earn profits but do not run the business and are not liable for its debts.
An amount of credit available for a specified time to a consumer that the consumer may access as he or she chooses; a standing loan available for a preset time period.
The power to reject some portions of a law without rejecting the whole thing. See also pocket veto.
Family descent or ancestry.
In a direct line from a particular relative, such as grandparent to parent to child; see also collateral.
A person who is directly descended in a straight line from an ancestor.
A person who inherits directly from a lineal ancestor or ascendant; see also descent.

A procedure used by police to identify criminal suspects, in which a suspect and several similar people are lined up in front of a wall and a witness is asked to identify the one who committed the crime.
To connect one website or computer with another. N. A place on a website that, if clicked, will connect the user to another website.
In cash or readily converted into cash; holding a large amount of cash and working capital.
Cash or items that can be quickly converted into cash.
(1) To convert assets into cash; to sell assets. (2) To settle; to pay debts; to settle the affairs of a business by determining debts and apportioning assets. N. liquidation.
A sum that a party to a contract agrees to pay if he or she breaches it; meant to be a reasonable estimate of the loss that the breach would cause. damages,
The amount of damages that a party to a contract agrees to pay if he or she breaches it.
The amount of cash available to a person or business; the ease with which a person or business can obtain cash.
(Latin) A pending lawsuit; a doctrine that requires purchasers of property or other people with an interest in property subject to a lawsuit to be bound by the outcome of the litigation. lis pendens,

A document containing several items of the same type, such as a court calendar or schedule, or a registry of voters. V. To compile a list; to register; to put a property up for sale with a real estate agent.
The publicly advertised price of an item.
Stock in a company that meets the Securities and Exchange Commission's requirements and is traded on an organized stock exchange; also called a listed security.
(1) Putting real estate up for sale; entering an agency agreement with a real estate agent to sell a piece of property. (2) Entering into a contract to place a company's stock on a stock exchange.
A party to a lawsuit; a plaintiff or defendant.
To bring a dispute to a court of law; to bring or defend a lawsuit in court.
A lawsuit or legal action; the act of bringing or defending a lawsuit. ADJ. litigious.
A lawyer who represents parties in lawsuits in courts of law; a specialist in litigation.
Compensation sufficient to support a family above poverty conditions. wage,
A wage sufficient to meet ordinary living expenses. See also minimum wage.
A document created by a person while still in relatively good mental condition stating in advance his or her wishes for medical treatment if and when he or she is no longer able to
Limited liability company.
(Latin) Legum Magister.
A group of private insurance underwriters based in London, England, that originally specialized in marine insurance but now will agree to underwrite a wide variety of risks.
Something that is borrowed, especially money; the act of lending something to someone, with the intention of having it returned at some future time. V. To lend; to borrow. loan,

A promise made by a bank or other lending institution to a prospective purchaser of real estate that it will lend him or her a specified sum at a specified interest rate to purchase a particular property if it is done within a specified period of time.
A person who lends money at extortionate interest rates, usually illegally.
An employee hired out to another employer for a particular job, who comes under the control of the temporary employer and for whom the temporary employer becomes liable.
The act of lending money at excessively high interest. See also extort, usury.
To attempt to influence a politician on a particular issue. N. A group of people who work to influence politicians on an issue; an attempt to influence politicians on an issue.
A person who works to influence politicians on particular issues and to persuade them to support or argue against particular items of proposed legislation.
The government of a geographical region smaller than a state, such as a city or county, with control over local matters.
Confining prisoners to their cells temporarily in order to restore security after a riot or other emergency.
An employer's refusal to allow employees to work during periods of negotiation; the employer's version of a strike.
(Latin) A site; a place.
The place where a contract was made.
The place where an offense occurred.
The place in which, i.e., the place where something occurred.

A method used by legislators to pass legislation without actually persuading fellow lawmakers of the validity of the proposed law, done by combining several unrelated matters into one bill in an effort to attract the votes of people who support individual parts of it, or by exchanging favors such as agreeing to vote for one another's bills.
To stand around idly; to move slowly and purposelessly. N. loitering.
A state statute that gives the state personal jurisdiction over nonresident defendants in matters that affect local residents and property.
Occurring over a long period of time.
A gain or loss that occurs from the sale of capital assets that have been held for the required length of time, usually at least one year.
Debt with a maturity date at least one year in the future.
A loan or mortgage that has a term of one year or more.

(1) A person who stands near the place where a crime is being committed to watch for police or other witnesses; a person stationed to watch for potential danger. (2) The watchfulness and diligence that a driver of an automobile must use while driving.
An ambiguity in a law; an inadequacy in a law that allows a person to legally avoid complying with it, especially in tax laws.
The fact or act of losing something; something that is lost; the detriment or damage resulting from the destruction or ruin of something; injury; expense. loss,
A cause of action that can be brought by someone whose spouse has been killed or injured, based on loss of material services, companionship, and sex.
Damage to a person's ability to earn money in the future.
Property that is no longer in its owner's possession due to the owner's negligence or carelessness and that cannot be found or recovered.
(1) An item or collection of items delivered or sold as one unit. (2) A plot of land. (3) A set of shares of stock to be traded; see also odd lot.
A game of chance in which people pay money for a chance to win a prize that is awarded at random.

The emotional support and kind feelings shared by close relatives such as parents and children or husbands and wives; love and affection can serve as adequate consideration for a gift between relatives.
An oath of allegiance to a government or other institution, required of some public officials.
Law School Admission Test.
A single payment of an entire amount due. See also installment.
The act of hiding in order to ambush someone or attack suddenly.
For a mob to kill someone for a suspected but unproven offense, usually by hanging, without the approval or authority of the law.
The treaty signed in Maastricht, the Netherlands, in 1992 that created the European Union.
Scheming or plotting, usually to carry out some evil purpose.

An 1803 U.S. Supreme Court case that formalized the concept of the judicial review of statutes, enshrining the principle that the courts could invalidate laws passed by legislatures if they found them unconstitutional.
A body of organized criminals who use extortion, threats, and violence to influence business or government; see also organized crime, mob.
A member of the mafia.
A public official with judicial, executive, or legislative power granted by the government, often functioning as a judge over minor matters or a justice of the peace.
A court over which a magistrate presides, handling small claims and minor matters.
(Latin) Great Charter; an agreement signed by King John in England in 1215, guaranteeing political rights and liberty to his nobles and forming the foundation of English rights and privileges.
A federal statute that requires warranties for consumer products to be written in clear, easily understandable language.

Letters or packages transported from sender to recipient through a postal system. V. To address a package or letter, place the proper postage on it, and deliver it into the keeping of a postal system.
Using letters sent through the mail to defraud people out of money.
A doctrine in contract law stating that an offer is accepted at the time a written acceptance is deposited in the mail.
To injure so as to cause permanent damage to some part of the body; to cripple someone.

(1) The person with the highest authority in some organization; the chief, such as the principal of a school. (2) A person who directs an agent to act on his or her behalf. (3) A person who commits a crime; the person directly responsible for a crime. (4) A sum of money invested or borrowed, on which interest is paid or charged.
To support; to continue; to keep something in a condition to continue and to prevent decline or decay; to continue to prosecute a lawsuit once beginning it.
(1) The upkeep of property. (2) Providing financial support for another person.
An opinion shared by a majority of the judges on a panel. opinion,
A form of government in which the desire of the largest group within the whole determines how the entire state, nation, or organization will act.
A vote in which the person who receives the greatest number of votes is declared the winner.

A person who makes or creates something; a person who creates a law; a person who signs a check or promissory note. malfeasance. Bad conduct; wrongdoing; a wrongful or unlawful act, particularly by a public official.
Evil intention; an intention to do a wrongful act or commit a crime without any justification or excuse.
A state of mind in which a person deliberately plans to commit a crime before actually performing it, characterized by cruelty and a disregard for the consequences to human life and social duties.
Intending to do harm; done out of ill will and without justification.
Intentionally using the legal process for an improper purpose.
Arresting on criminal charges a person who is known not to have committed a crime or arresting a person without probable cause.
Criminal or civil litigation brought without probable cause and out of malice; if the defendant prevails in such a case, he or she may bring an action for the tort of malicious prosecution.
To pretend to be sick or disabled to get out of work or to keep receiving disability compensation.
A person who malingers.
Incompetence or improper conduct by a professional performing professional duties. malpractice,
(Latin) Bad in itself; describes an act that is inherently wrong in itself without regard to what the law says.
(Latin) Bad because it is prohibited; describes an act that is wrong because it is prohibited by law.
(Latin) We command; a writ issued by a superior court to a lower court, corporation, or officer, ordering it to do some act that is a duty required of it by law.

(1) A judicial order or command directing that some action be taken, especially from a higher court to a lower one. (2) Authority granted to a person to do some act, usually granted to an elected official by the electorate. V. (1) To require that something be done. (2) To give someone authority to do something.
A sentence set by law for a particular offense, with no room for judicial discretion.
Evident;

A public declaration of political principles issued by a sovereign or politician; a formal document issued by an executive officer of a state declaring its reasons for taking some international action.

To influence someone unfairly; to control someone's actions; to buy or sell a security in order to give a false impression about its value and thus influence the purchasing or selling decisions of others. N.
manipulative.
A federal statute that makes it a crime to transport a woman or girl across state lines for immoral purposes such as prostitution; also called the White Slave Traffic Act.
The unlawful and unjustifiable killing of another person without premeditation or malice aforethought. manslaughter,

(1) An edge or border; the place where water meets land. (2) The difference between two prices, such as the selling price of an item and its cost of production. (3) The amount paid to a securities broker by a customer when using the broker's credit to buy securities. ADJ. marginal.
The purchasing of securities using a combination of cash and a loan extended by the broker making the purchase.
Cannabis; a plant whose leaves can be smoked as an illegal drug; also spelled marihuana; also called pot, grass, weed, Mary Jane.
Related to marriage.
An agreement or contract between a married couple or a couple about to get married, usually stating which partner owns what marital property.

The doctrine that a person does not have to disclose confidential communications made by his or her spouse during a legal marriage, and that such communications are inadmissible at trial; see also husband-wife privilege.
Property acquired by either spouse during a marriage, which is subject to equitable division if the parties divorce unless they made a marital agreement that allocated it; also called marital estate.
The condition of being single, married, divorced, separated, or widowed.
Related to the sea and seafaring; connected with all bodies of navigable water and commerce upon them.
The body of laws governing travel and commerce on navigable waters.

(1) A place where goods and services are bought and sold. (2) The demand for a particular good or service; the state of commerce in general. (3) The actions of supply and demand on commerce; the free market.
The price for an item that a buyer will pay and a seller will accept on the open market; the current price for an item; also called market value.
Title to property that is free from encumbrances such that a reasonable person would accept it, and that can be sold or transferred without impediment.
The world of commerce and trade.
A legally recognized union between a man and a woman as husband and wife. V. marry. marriage,
A document certifying that a couple is legally married.
A document issued by the state authorizing two people to marry within a stated period of time.
A public officer who enforces laws; the head of a police or fire department; a sheriff. V. To arrange; to put in order. See also United States Marshal.
Arranging assets into the order in which they will be used to pay debts.
Government by the military instead of civilians during times of war or extreme civil unrest.

(1) A person with authority over another; an employer. (2) The presiding officer in a court of equity, also called a master in chancery or master in equity. (3) An officer appointed to represent a court in some matter, also called a special master.

The relationship between an employer and an employee in which the employee performs services for the employer in return for pay, and the employer directs the employee and has the right to fire him or her.
The long-term plan for some governmental project, such as the plan for the urban development of a city.
A rule stating that an employer is responsible for conduct by an employee while the employee is working for him or her.
Essential; important; relevant to establishing a cause of action or arriving at a judgment; see also immaterial. N. materiality.
A change to a document that alters its original meaning or its legal effect.
Evidence that influences the judge's or jury's perception of the facts in a case; evidence with a real bearing on the issues.

A fact that is relevant and important to the matter at hand and that would influence the actions of other parties if made known to them; a fact that would cause someone to decide to enter or not enter a contract.
A witness who is the only person, or one of very few people, who can testify about a particular fact.
See lien, mechanic's. lien,
Having to do with mothers and motherhood.
A line of descent traced through mothers.
The condition of motherhood. See also paternity.
Leave granted a worker during pregnancy, or after the birth or adoption of a new child.
The killing of a mother by her child; a person who kills his or her own mother.
A lawsuit related to the marital status of the parties, such as an action for divorce, separation, or annulment.
The state of marriage; the ceremony in which two people are married. ADJ. matrimonial.
A situation or state of affairs; the facts that comprise a cause of action or defense.
The situation that is the basis of a lawsuit.
The factual issues to be ascertained at trial; also called a question of fact.
A question of the proper law to apply to the facts of a case, to be determined by the judge; also called a question of law.
An issue that can be proven by the official record on which it has been entered.
Fully developed;
The date on which a note or other commercial obligation comes due.
The amount due for a mature note or other obligation.
A short statement of a principle or rule of law, often used in deciding equity cases.
(1) Violent disorder or chaos. (2) The crime of maliciously injuring, maiming, or dismembering someone so as to render the victim permanently unable to fight.

The Bipartisan Campaign Reform Act of 2002, a federal law that sets rules for campaign finance, limiting the amounts of money that contributors can donate and regulating the types of advertising that campaigns may use to raise money. means. N. (1) Resources, especially financial. (2) The action or method that allows something to happen.

A lien that allows builders, tradesmen, suppliers, and others who provide materials or labor for construction or maintenance of buildings to attach the land or buildings in question to secure payment for their work and materials. lien,
To intervene in a dispute in order to help the parties resolve it.

A form of alternate dispute resolution in which a neutral third party, the mediator, hears the testimony of both parties to a dispute and tries to help them agree on a solution, but cannot impose a decision on them.

A health insurance program run by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services that pays for health care for low-income individuals, the disabled, and several other categories that people may fall under.
Failure by a physician to use the degree of skill or care that should ordinarily be exercised in a situation.
A health insurance program run by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services that pays for health care for people who are at least 65 years old and certain other individuals.
The state of agreement about the terms and conditions of a contract that the parties to it must reach for it to be validly created.
A person or business that belongs to a larger group or organization.
A bank that is affiliated with the Federal Reserve System.
A securities brokerage firm that belongs to a particular stock exchange.

(1) An informal document that records the details of a transaction, event, or agreement, or discusses some matter. (2) A document that examines the facts of a case and applies the law to it to see whether it has merit or not, usually written by a law clerk or low-level attorney at the request of a higher-level attorney, and considered attorney work product; also called a memorandum of law or a memo.
A legal document that expresses an agreement between two parties but that is not as formal or binding as a contract. ABBRV. MOU.
To threaten,

The mental state that occurs when committing a crime, i.e., guilt, criminal intent, or knowledge that a crime is being committed; one of the four mental states in which a crime may be committed, i.e., intentionally, knowingly, recklessly, or negligently. mens rea,
Extreme distress, anxiety, and any other mental pain that is extreme enough to merit damages for the victim.
A person's ability to understand what he or she is doing or the effects that other people's actions will have on him or her; see also capacity.
In marriage, behavior by one spouse that endangers the mental health of the other or is so humiliating and unpleasant that it makes the other's life miserable.
Related to merchants, trade, and commerce; commercial.
Commercial law; the law that governs merchants, trade, and commerce.
A person who buys and sells goods, usually buying wholesale and selling at retail; a trader; a person who imports and exports goods for sale.
Salable; able to be sold; of the type described and fit for the purpose for which it is sold. N. merchantability.
A free trade agreement between Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, and several other South American nations that are associate members.
Euthanasia; killing a person or animal because continued life would be intolerable due to a painful, incurable illness or severe injury.
To combine two or more things together into one whole.

(1) The combination of two or more entities, rights, or other things into a single whole, especially the absorption of the minor entities, rights, or things into the largest of them. (2) The combining of two or more corporations into one, with one of them absorbing the others into itself. (3) The absorption of one estate into another when one person comes into possession of both of them. (4) Sentencing a person convicted of two or more crimes for only one of them. (5) After a plaintiff receives judgment in his or her favor, the combination of that judgment with other possible
The legal substance of a claim or defense.
Intermediate; in the middle.
(1) Profits occurring between two points in time. (2) Profits earned from land by a person who has no right to it, recoverable by the landowner.
A type of Internet forum in which users can post and respond to messages on various topics.
Codes written in a computer language such as HTML or XML that identify particular types of text or images on a web page or document.
Boundaries of property; a method of measuring and describing land boundaries using distances and angles from surrounding properties and landmarks.
Most favored nation.

An antitrust lawsuit brought by the U.S. Department of Justice against Microsoft Corporation in 1998, in which the United States alleged that Microsoft had violated antimonopoly laws by bundling its operating system and Web browser software, thus unfairly preventing rivals from competing for customers.
Related to war, the armed forces, and soldiers.

As listed in the Constitution, the three kinds of military authority over civil matters: military law, which governs the military during peacetime and war; military government, in which a military commander appointed by the president supersedes civilian laws during times of civil and foreign war; and, martial law, ordered by the president during times of dire emergencies.
The branch of law that regulates the military and applies only to members of the armed services.

An organization of citizens, civilians or military that defends a locality, state, or nation, but that is not part of a standing army; an organization of so-called citizen soldiers, such as the National Guard.
An opening made in the earth to allow the extraction of minerals.
A solid inorganic substance naturally occurring in the earth, often obtained by mining, drilling, or quarrying, such as coal, oil, gas, iron, gold, uranium, etc.
The right to take minerals out of a particular piece of land.
The lowest hourly wage an employer is allowed to pay workers in most jobs, set by statute.

The minimum level of contact with a forum state that a nonresident defendant must have to be subjected to personal jurisdiction in the courts of that state without offending due process of law; usually intentional business activity in the forum state is enough.

The federally established lowest hourly wage that employers engaged in interstate commerce must pay their employees, or the equivalent established by an individual state, set at a level to allow workers to maintain their health and well-being.
The process or business of extracting minerals from the earth.
A lease that allows the lessee to enter land to search for minerals on it and to extract them if found.

(1) A member of the clergy, particularly of Protestant Christian denominations. (2) The head of a governmental department. (3) A diplomatic official who represents one state or nation to another. (4) (Archaic) An agent; someone who acts for someone else. V. To care for; to meet someone's needs.
An act done by an agent or subordinate who is acting on orders from a superior.
A person below the age of majority; a child or juvenile. ADJ. Small; of less importance.

(1) The smaller group or number of a whole; a number less than half of a total; the opposite of majority. (2) The condition of being a minor; childhood.
