The tort of intentional infliction of severe emotional distress; an extreme insult or violent injury.
The practice of a business hiring another company or outside individuals to perform some of its work; subcontracting. See also independent contractor.
Undone; remaining to be done or paid.
The money still to be paid on a debt.
A check that has been written and given to its recipient but has not yet been deducted from a bank account.
A securities market of dealers who buy and sell securities that are not listed in a securities exchange, done primarily over the telephone or online.
Used to describe a statute that covers too many situations, i.e., that, while legitimately forbidding certain conduct,
The condition of drawing more money out of an account than it has in it, such as by writing a check for too great an amount.
Not having been done or paid by the appointed date; delayed.
Operating expenses of a business aside from cost of materials or labor.
Using fraud or a superior bargaining position to take advantage of someone else in a commercial transaction.
To reverse or annul; to overturn or void the decision of another court.
Open; visible; not concealed.
In criminal law, an open act done as part of a crime.
Time worked in addition to normal working hours.
Wages paid for overtime worked, usually at a higher rate than ordinary hourly pay.
To possess; to hold title to property.
A person or business that has legal title to property.
The state of owning property, with a right to possess, use, and dispose of it.
A word meaning "hear ye," cried by a bailiff to mark the beginning of a court session; pronounced "oh yes."
usage_examples: cried by a bailiff to mark the beginning of a court session; pronounced

definition: A portion of the atmosphere surrounding the Earth that contains a high concentration of ozone, a molecule that plays an important role in absorbing excess ultraviolet radiation from the sun. overtime pay 356
A person who thinks war and violence are always unjustifiable, and who refuses to participate in war or military activities. See also conscientious objector.
An agreement between two or more parties.
A corporation's surplus obtained by selling stock at a price above par value.
Physical and mental distress or injury, for which damages may be recovered in a tort action.
Financial support similar to alimony given to a partner in a terminated nonmarital relationship; see also domestic partner.
To cater to someone's immoral lusts and desires, such as by providing pornography; to procure a prostitute for someone. N. A pimp.
A group of people joined together for some united purpose, such as a group of judges who sit together to hear the same case, or the group of jurors summoned to court for jury duty.
A document, including letters, contracts, account books, promissory notes, and anything else that can be written or printed on paper; a written or printed instrument.
Banknotes; documents printed by a government, backed by its credit, and used as currency.
Profit or loss that exists on the books but has not been converted into cash.
(1) A standard or norm used as a benchmark for comparison, such as the value of one nation's currency compared to
A person with some legal training who assists an attorney in preparing cases, documents, and other legal tasks.
Most important; of highest rank; supreme.
The superior of two titles to the same property, i.e., the one that will prevail over the other.
A lover, usually of a married person committing adultery.
(1) Equipment used for a particular activity. (2) (Archaic) A married woman's own personal property, apart from a dowry, that she could keep after the death of her husband.
(1) A package. (2) A quantity of something, such as a piece of land or an estate. V. To divide something into portions and distribute them to various recipients.

To forgive; to release a convicted criminal from liability for his or her crime; to absolve. N. An act by a governor, president, or other government official releasing a convicted criminal from punishment for a crime and reinstating his or her former level of civil liberties.
(Latin) Parent of the country; the doctrine that it is the role of the government to look out for those who cannot take care of themselves.
(1) A father or mother, natural or adoptive. (2) A company or organization that controls several subsidiaries,
Parents' liability for the actions of their minor children.
A parent's rights in regard to his or her children, including the right to custody, discipline, control over a child's earnings and property, and to be supported by a grown child.
(1) An administrative district of a Christian church. (2) In Louisiana, a territorial division equivalent to a county.

(1) Equality, particularly of pay or status; equivalence of goods. (2) A method of regulating prices for farm goods by making them equivalent to prices set on a particular date. (3) One country's value in terms of another's at a particular rate of exchange. See also par.
A legislature, such as the highest legislature of the United Kingdom. ADJ. parliamentary.
Rules governing procedure in a legislature; also called parliamentary procedure.
A work such as a film, literary work, or song that imitates another work in a humorous, ironic, or satirical way.
Verbal or spoken evidence; spoken testimony by a witness.
A rule that prevents the parties to a written agreement from altering it with spoken provisions or oral agreements.
The release of a convict from prison before the end of his or her sentence on the condition that the former prisoner follow certain rules and commit no more crimes.
An official who supervises parolees.
A person released from prison on parole.
A verdict that finds a criminal defendant guilty of one or more charges and innocent of the others. verdict,
Loss of only part of something, as opposed to total loss.
To take part in something; to do something with other people; to share. N. participation.
Associated with and espousing the views of a particular political party.
To divide into sections. N. The division of property between co-owners so that each gets an individual interest in a part of it.

(1) A person who shares the profits and risks of some business or other enterprise with one or several other people. (2) A spouse or domestic partner. V. To join with someone else as partners. partner,
An association between two or more people who call themselves partners and share the profits and risks of a business or some other undertaking. partnership,

(1) A person or entity involved in some transaction or matter; a person or entity on one side of a lawsuit or other dispute. (2) An organized political group that tries to make its own views the law of the land by participating in elections and working for common goals in government; also called a political party. See also third party.
A wall built on the border between two pieces of property that is shared equally by both owners.
A trust that does not require any action by the trustee. trust,
A document issued by a national government that identifies its bearer as a citizen of that country with permission to travel abroad and return home under the home nation's protection.
A security checkpoint at an entrance point into a country where officials inspect and approve the passports of those wishing to enter.

(1) An exclusive right granted by the government to manufacture and sell an invention for a specified period of time. (2) A document granting land from a government to a person. V. To obtain a patent for an invention. ADJ. Obvious; readily apparent.
The federal agency that issues patents and trademarks as part of the Department of Commerce.
A notice placed on an object informing others that its inventor has applied for a patent on it and is awaiting the government's decision.
An individual or business that acquires patents without planning to manufacture the patented items but intending to sue anyone who infringes on the patents.
Having to do with fatherhood and fathers; showing the care expected of a father.
Fatherhood; the state of being a father. See also maternity.
A lawsuit brought to identify the father of a child and demand child support payments from him.

A federal law requiring health care facilities to inform patients of their rights under state law to create advance directives to dictate how they should be cared for if they become incapacitated; see also living will, durable power of attorney.

A statement of patients' rights adopted by many hospitals and health care providers, and made law by the federal government and several states, that covers topics such as confidentiality, consent to treatment, and dignity.
A privilege exempting confidential communications between patients and their doctors from discovery.
The killing of one's own father; a person who kills his or her father.
Property inherited from one's father; property inherited from either parent or other ancestors; heritage.
(1) A person who supports or protects someone or something, usually with financial contributions. (2) A regular customer of a business. V. patronize.
(1) The support given by a patron. (2) Regular business given by customers to a store, theater, etc. patronage,
A destitute person; a person so poverty-stricken that he or she must be supported by the public. See also indigent.
To give an object of personal property to another person, called a pawnbroker, as security for a loan.
To give money in exchange for goods or services, or to discharge a debt. N. Compensation; wages; payment.
Due to be paid; required to be paid.
The amount paid for something; the act of paying for something; satisfaction of a debt or obligation.
Payment to the holder of a negotiable instrument on or after its maturity date in good faith and with no known defects in title.
A party's depositing with the court money or other property while a lawsuit is in progress, after which the court will pay out the money and property according to the judgment or other settlement.
A taxable payment, such as a dividend, drawn from a company's earnings and made by that company to its shareholders.
A list of all the employees in a company and the amounts they are paid; the total amount a company pays its employees.
Taxes paid on wages, salary, or income, including income tax, Social Security tax, and unemployment tax.
Peaceful; free from arguments, conflict, or violence.
Continuous possession of property without any adverse claims on it.
For a public officer to steal public funds entrusted to him or her; to embezzle. N. peculation.
Related to money; financial.
A bequest of money.
A loss of money or any anticipated economic benefit, such as a child's support by a parent.
A device that records the numbers dialed on a telephone; see also eavesdrop, wiretapping.
Related to punishment or penalties.
The codification of criminal law; criminal statutes. penal institution. A correctional institution, such as a prison, jail, or penitentiary.
To impose a punishment on someone.
A punishment.
A clause in a contract or agreement that describes a penalty for a breach, default, or other infraction, usually not enforceable by a court.
Discretionary jurisdiction by a federal court over a state law claim that arises out of the same matter that simultaneously creates a federal claim.
(Latin) Pending the lawsuit; describes matters that must be put on hold to await the outcome of a lawsuit that will determine how they are settled.
About to happen; begun but not yet completed; awaiting settlement.
A prison, usually for the incarceration of people convicted of felonies or serious crimes.
A regular payment made to a person after retirement by his or her former employer or by the government, usually from a fund to which the person contributed while working.
A plan established by an employer to provide retirement benefits to employees.
(Latin) By heads; according to the number of people;
(Latin) By the court; by a court's or judge's decision.
(Latin) In itself; intrinsically; by its very nature.

(Latin) By roots or by shoots, as of a tree; a form of distribution of an estate in which each recipient takes a share according to the proportion that would have fallen to his or her deceased ancestor; the opposite of per capita. E.g., Grandmother has three daughters, A, B, and C. A is still alive but B and C are dead. A has one child, B has one child, and C has four children. If Grandmother dies and leaves $300,000, under a per stirpes arrangement, A will inherit $100,000 and her child will inherit nothing; B's child will take his dead mother's share of $100,000; and C's four children will divide their mother's share of $100,000, each receiving $25,000.
Absolute; final; indisputable.
A principle of international law that is so important and so widely accepted that no state is permitted to deviate from it, such as the prohibitions on genocide, slavery, or piracy.
Complete; without flaw or defect; having fulfilled the requirements to make something enforceable at law. V. To make perfect; to finish; to fulfill all requirements necessary for some transaction.
Treachery.
To do some act in fulfillment of a promise; to take the required steps to meet an obligation; to accomplish something. N. performance.
A bond given as surety by a building contractor to a customer to ensure that the contractor will complete the project, and that the customer may use to pay for completion if the contractor defaults.
Danger; risk; the risk that is covered by an insurance policy.
Occurring regularly at intervals.
Alimony paid in a specific amount at intervals, such as monthly; also called permanent alimony if it lasts for the recipient spouse's lifetime.
A tenancy that lasts for a specified period, such as a year, and that cannot be terminated except at the end of the rental period.
The crime of intentionally lying under oath during a judicial proceeding, such as at trial.
Fixed; continuing or lasting without interruption; intended to stay in the same place or the same condition indefinitely.
A fixed residence; a long-term home that the resident intends to stay in indefinitely.
A disability that impairs a person's earning capacity for the rest of his or her life.
Employment that will continue until terminated by either employee or employer.
Being allowed to do something; authorization.
Allowed but optional.
A counterclaim that a defendant is allowed to bring regarding a matter that is completely different from the one brought by the plaintiff in the original complaint. See also joinder, use, waste.
To authorize or consent to something; to allow. N. An official document authorizing someone to do something.
To commit a crime or other wrongful act. N. perpetration.
A person who commits a crime.
Never ending; continuing without interruption or end.

(1) Something that lasts forever; the condition of lasting forever. (2) A restriction on property that makes it impossible to transfer forever, or for a period beyond that allowed by law; an estate with such a restriction on it. See also rule against perpetuities.
The fringe benefits and special rights and privileges that go with a position; also called perks.
A human being; an individual, corporation, partnership, trustee, labor organization, government, or other entity with defined legal responsibilities and rights.
(Latin) A person who is not pleasing; an unwelcome, undesirable, or unwanted person.
Actual delivery of the document to the person named on it. service,
Movable property and chattels owned by an individual, particularly all goods owned by a person at the time of death; also called personal property or personalty.
A company with few shareholders and a large percentage of income through passive sources such as rents or royalties.
Physical injury to a person's body; also refers to a branch of law specializing in torts arising out of physical injuries.
A lawyer who specializes in bringing lawsuits on behalf of plaintiffs who have suffered physical injuries.
Liability for a matter extending to an individual's personal funds and property.
A person who handles the affairs of someone who cannot do so himor herself, such as a child, an incapacitated person, or a deceased person; see also executor.
To use arguments and reasoning to induce someone to adopt a particular opinion on a matter. N. persuasion.

term: pertaining to the creation of laws
definition: legislative act, legislative branch, legislative history, legislative immunity, legislative power.
(French) Small; see also petty.

A trial jury that has the job of hearing testimony, seeing evidence, and using the information to determine issues of fact and reach a conclusion about them, such as a verdict in a criminal case. See also grand jury.
(1) A person who presents a petition to some authority. (2) A person who brings a legal action by means of a petition either in a court of equity or a court of appeals.
Small; unimportant; trivial; an English corruption of the French word "petit."

Using email and misleading websites to entice users to provide their credit card numbers, bank account numbers, passwords, usernames, and other confidential information in order to use that information for criminal purposes such as theft.

A group of people carrying signs and standing or marching outside a place of business in a peaceful protest against working conditions, labor practices, union grievances, or other sources of disagreement. V. To participate in a picket.
The act of participating in a picket.
To impose personal liability for corporate activities on individuals associated with the corporation, doing away with the protection against personal liability that ordinarily
A person who finds clients for prostitutes in exchange for a share of their earnings. V. To work as a pimp. See also pander.
(1) The practice of attacking and robbing ships at sea; see also hijack. (2) The unlawful copying and distribution of copyrighted works such as software or recordings of music.
A person who commits acts of piracy.
A place where a business is run or where someone works; see also domicile. place of business,
To copy someone else's words or ideas and pass them off as one's own. N. plagiarism. See also copyright.
An obvious error made by a trial court that substantially affects a defendant's rights and warrants a reversal of the judgment and a new trial.

An interpretation of a statute arrived at by reading its words and interpreting them according to their general, common meaning, usually without considering legislative history or other evidence to determine the intent of the people who drafted it.

Those things that can be immediately seen without special effort because they are sitting out in the open and are unconcealed; incriminating evidence in plain view can be taken by police without a search warrant.
A person who files a complaint to start a lawsuit.
An appellant; one who brings an appeal; "appellant" is the term more commonly used today.
An attorney who specializes in representing plaintiffs.
A law firm that specializes in bringing lawsuits on behalf of plaintiffs, usually for a contingency fee. See also defense firm.
A benefits plan or health plan; a package of employee benefits.
The one-year period in which a given benefits package remains in place, after which members and plan components may be modified.
A map showing the property lines, roads, and other landmarks of an area; also called a plot.

(1) In criminal law, a defendant's response to the charges brought against him or her. (2) Formerly, at common law or equity, one of several kinds of pleading that have now been made obsolete under the Rules of Civil Procedure.
An agreement made between a criminal defendant and the prosecutor in which the defendant agrees to plead guilty to a lesser offense in exchange for a lighter punishment after conviction.
(1) In criminal law, to answer charges brought by the prosecution. (2) To make or file a pleading in a lawsuit; to file a pleading in response to a plaintiff's complaint.

A document containing a party's side of a lawsuit, such as a plaintiff's complaint or a defendant's answer, in which the party lists the facts that support his or her side of the case and presents them to the court at the beginning of a lawsuit. pleading,
(1) A promise. (2) An object given as security for a promise, contract, debt, or other obligation.
A person who receives a pledge as security.
A person who gives a pledge to someone else.
Absolute; complete; full.
A full trial on the merits; see also summary.
A meeting of a legislature or other group attended by all members. Plessy v.

(1) In voting for more than two candidates, the number of votes for the candidate who wins more than any other but does not receive a majority. (2) On an appellate court with a panel of judges, an opinion joined by the greatest number of
Government by the wealthy; a group of wealthy people who control the government.
A wealthy person who uses his or her wealth to gain political power.

A president's or governor's method of rejecting a bill submitted by the legislature toward the end of a legislative session by simply refusing to accept or reject it and letting it die through inaction, rather than rejecting it outright with a veto.

(1) An argument; a distinct proposition or issue of law; the relevance or reason of some argument. (2) In real estate, a fee of 1% of a loan amount paid to the lender when the loan is created. (3) In securities, a measure of value, often worth one dollar.
A difficult legal issue set aside at trial for later consideration so that the court can proceed with other issues.
An organization maintained by a government to preserve public order, prevent crimes, and apprehend criminals. V. To maintain order; to enforce laws.
A local or municipal court with jurisdiction over minor criminal matters.
A government's power to preserve the peace and public order through the use of a police force and criminal laws that restrain personal freedom and property rights.
(1) The principles that guide the actions of a government, business, or other entity or individual. (2) A contract of insurance. (3) A lottery.
A person who owns an insurance policy.
Related to government and public affairs.
A question that a court will not decide because it should be decided by another branch of government, such as the legislature.
Any geographical area that is governed by a particular governmental entity, such as a county or school district.
A person engaged in politics, particularly one elected or running for public office.
The activities of a government and those associated with it.

(1) To record votes or opinions from people. (2) To ask the members of a jury individually what their own decision is before the jury's verdict is recorded. N. A record of votes cast in an election; a place where votes are cast (also called polls); a list of voters.
A tax paid by a person in exchange for the right to vote in elections; a tax on individuals of a particular class, such as all males over 21 years of age.
Simultaneous marriage to more than one person.
A lie detector; a device that monitors heart rate, breathing, and other physical aspects of a person being questioned

A kind of pyramid scheme in which a perpetrator promises high returns on an investment and uses money submitted by later investors to pay off earlier investors, but eventually runs out of money or disappears and the scheme collapses. See also Securities and Exchange Commission.

(1) A group of people or organizations, especially those involved in the same business, who combine resources to benefit one another and to reduce competition. (2) A common fund contributed to and shared by a number of people,
An advertisement that opens a new window under the active window when the user visits a particular website. See also banner ad.
An advertisement that opens in a new window on top of the active window when a user visits a particular website.
Literature, photography, or other printed or visual material that depicts sex organs or sexual activity,
A harbor; a place where ships can load and unload, and where customs and duties are collected; a city or town with such a harbor.
The port or airport from which a ship or aircraft begins its journey.
A place where a ship, aircraft, or other vessel, and its passengers or cargo, enter a country, where customs and duties are paid and immigration procedures observed.
The collective investments held by a person or institution.
Law created by a government and human institution to run society; see also natural law.
The belief that laws exist to run society and are valid because they are enacted by humans, and ideals or worries about justice should not limit their application.

To own; to hold without owning; to control something; to occupy physically. possession. (1) The act of holding or controlling something; having visible control over something; physically occupying a property. (2) A belonging; something that is held or owned.
A lawsuit in which the plaintiff's objective is to recover possession of real property, as opposed to recovering title to it.
The right to possess property, i.e., to occupy it or control it and to exclude others from it.
Something that could happen but will not necessarily happen; a contingent interest in property.
A chance that an estate will return to the person who granted it if certain conditions occur.

(1) Mail; letters and packages and the system that delivers them. (2) A job; an assigned duty or position, such as that held by a soldier. (3) A military base. V. (1) To mail a letter or package. (2) To hang a sign or notice in some public place, such as a sign warning away trespassers from property. (3) To enter an amount in
(Latin) After this, therefore because of this; the mistaken reasoning that if something happens after an event, it was necessarily caused by that event.
After conviction of a criminal offense.
Court actions that allow a criminal defendant to challenge a conviction on constitutional grounds; ABBRV. PCR actions.
To enter a date on an instrument that is later than the date on which it is written.
After marriage.
An agreement made between spouses settling their property rights if they should divorce or if one should die. See also prenuptial agreement.
After death.
A child born after the death of his or her father.

(1) A place for the detention of illegally parked automobiles or stray animals. (2) A unit of weight used in the United States and the United Kingdom. (3) The basic unit of currency in the United Kingdom and several other nations.
A provision in a will directing that estate property be placed in a previously established trust, or that property be moved from a trust to a will.
Authority; the ability or liberty to do something. power,

term: power exerted to make something happen
definition: To make someone do something against his or her will. force,
The power held by a person who receives an offer to create a contract by accepting it.

Authority given by a donor or testator allowing a donee to choose the beneficiaries of a fund, trust, or estate; title to the transferred property passes directly from the donor or testator to the beneficiaries chosen by the donee. power of appointment,
The authority to act for someone else in legal matters; a document conferring that authority to someone.
Possible under given circumstances; feasible in actual practice.

(1) Customary action or procedure; the established procedures of law and court proceedings. (2) The exercise of a profession or religion. V. To carry out the daily activities of a profession such as law or medicine, or of a religion; to work as a professional in a particular field.
A formal request in a complaint or petition in equity asking for relief for the plaintiff's grievance; also called a prayer for relief or demand for relief. V. pray.

A report on a convicted criminal including employment history, education, prior arrest and conviction history, family background, and sentencing recommendations intended to help the trial court set a suitable sentence.
An introductory statement; an introductory section of a statute, constitution, etc., that explains the reason for its creation and the objects it is meant to achieve.
Uncertain; likely to break, fall, or fail; not securely held.
A loan that must be repaid whenever the lender demands it; a loan that is likely not to be repaid.
Expressing a request, wish, or recommendation, as when a testator expresses a wish in his or her will.
A previously decided case that serves as a guide for deciding subsequent cases that have similar facts or legal questions. See also condition precedent.
(1) A moral rule. (2) A written order, warrant, or writ.

(1) To act before someone else in order to prevent him or her from doing something; to forestall someone else's action. (2) In property, to settle or cultivate land before anyone else in order to acquire a right to purchase it and exclude other settlers; to purchase something before someone else has the chance to purchase it.

(1) Acting before someone else; forestalling. (2) Purchasing something before someone else has the opportunity to do so. (3) A doctrine holding that federal laws take precedence over state laws in certain situations, and that states may not pass laws that are inconsistent with federal ones.
The right of someone who already holds shares of stock in a company to purchase additional shares of a new issue before they are made available to others.
(1) To place before others; to treat one person better than another; to like one thing better than another. (2) To prosecute; to submit a charge, such as a criminal charge; to bring an indictment.
(1) Favor shown to one person above others. (2) Placing one creditor in front of others; paying one creditor to the exclusion of or harm to other creditors.
Favoring one person or group over another.
A debt that will be paid before others.
A workplace in which union members receive better treatment than nonunion employees.
Promotion to a position.
Favored; receiving better treatment than other similar people or objects.
A creditor who receives payment ahead of other creditors of the same debtor.
Stock that received dividends or distributions ahead of common stock.
Bias; prejudgment not based on actual experience or evidence; injury to a party that results from preconceived notions about the facts. V. To cause prejudice; to harm. ADJ. prejudicial.
Initial; introductory; preparatory to something more important.
An initial hearing on a criminal charge to give the judge a chance to determine whether the prosecution has enough evidence to bring the charge to trial.
To consider an act before doing it; to think out and plan a crime before committing it. N. premeditation.

A logical proposition or assertion forming the basis of a subsequent assertion; in a legal document such as a contract, the introductory language that explains why the document has been created. V. To base an argument on a proposition.
Land and the buildings on it.

(1) A reward given for services rendered; a bonus; a sum added to wages or interest. (2) The fee paid for an insurance policy. ADJ. Superior; of better quality and usually of a higher price than ordinary goods.
Before birth; related to or during pregnancy.
Before marriage.
An agreement signed by a couple before they marry arranging for ownership of assets owned individually by each of them before marriage. See also post-nuptial agreement.
The usual standard of proof in civil cases, in which the evidence of one side is more likely to be true than the evidence of the opposing side; evidence that is more probable than not.
A right or privilege that is exclusive to a person, office, or class.
A writ issued by a court through its discretionary powers, not in common use under the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

(1) To direct, guide, or recommend; to order that some action be taken. (2) To claim the right to use something based on a history of having used it for a long time; to claim an easement to land on the basis of having used the land for a period of time already.

(1) A recommendation or order given by some authority, such as a doctor's prescription. (2) The acquisition of an easement through continuous use of some property; such an easement is called a prescriptive easement.
An easement that arises in a manner similar to adverse possession by long use of the servient estate. easement,

(1) A written accusation of a crime created and signed by the members of a grand jury and presented to the court. (2) The act of presenting a negotiable instrument to the person responsible for paying it in order to receive payment.
To hold the position of authority in a gathering or meeting; to be in charge of something; to sit in authority over a court.
(1) A person with authority over others; one who presides. (2) An elected head of a republic.
To suppose that something is true based on available evidence; to assume or infer that something is the case.

The act of presuming; the use of existing facts to infer other facts that are assumed to be true until they are rebutted; an assumption that must be made by a court if certain facts are shown and that will stand until other facts are presented to rebut it. presumption,
In criminal law, the assumption that a person is innocent until proven guilty.
The assumption that a child born to a married woman is the offspring of her legal husband.
Presumed, inferred, or supposed; giving reason to assume the existence of a fact.
Evidence that is accepted as true until rebutted by contradictory evidence.
To omit, especially to omit a child or heir from a will, usually in cases where the child or heir was not born when the will was created. N. pretermission.
Before trial begins.

A conference held by the court between the beginning of a lawsuit or criminal prosecution and the commencement of trial in which the attorneys for both sides define issues, discuss discovery, take other steps to make the trial run smoothly, and possibly settle the dispute.
A program under which minor criminal defendants are not convicted, but instead sent straight to probation where they are given an opportunity to correct their behavior.
(1) To win. (2) To be common or widespread.
The party to a lawsuit who receives judgment in his or her favor.
To speak evasively; to act in a deceitful manner. N. prevarication.
Confinement of a criminal defendant before trial without the option of bail in cases where the defendant would pose an immediate risk to the public if released pending trial.
The amount of money asked or paid for something; the cost of some object or service. V. To set a price.
Asking different prices from different buyers for the same item.
Conspiring with other individuals or companies in the same business to set prices instead of letting them be set by market forces, usually to restrict competition. price fixing,
A privilege exempting confidential communications made between a priest and a confessor in the confessional from discovery.
(Latin) First face; at first sight; based on first impressions; the initial view of something, accepted as true until disproven.
An initial case; a case with sufficient proof to stand trial and withstand a motion to dismiss or for a directed verdict, which will be accepted as true until the defendant proves otherwise.
Evidence sufficient to establish a claim or defense until rebutted by contrary evidence.
First; principal;
The best and most important evidence in a case.
The principle that where an administrative agency has jurisdiction over a dispute, the courts will not hear the case until the agency has heard it first.
First; most important.
A general contractor; the contractor who is ultimately responsible for an entire project and the work of subcontractors.
The true price paid for something; the true cost of an item, including the material and labor used to produce it.
The lowest interest rate for short-term unsecured loans charged by a lender to its customers with the best credit.

(1) The condition of being a firstborn child. (2) The right of succession that belongs to a firstborn child; in feudal times, the eldest son's right to inherit the entire estate of his father, leaving nothing for younger sons.
Most important;
A fundamental truth, especially a moral truth, that serves as a basis for action; a rule of action or procedure.
Existing or occurring before something else; preceding.
A statement made earlier outside of court by a witness that contradicts his or her statement made on the witness stand.
A restriction placed on a kind of free expression before it has actually been used, e.g., a prohibition placed on a certain kind of publication before anyone has created such a publication.
Precedence; something that is ranked higher than something else; the condition of being more important than someone or something else.
A jail or penitentiary; a building used to house convicted criminals.
A person confined to a prison; a person confined against his or her will.
A person who is being tried for a crime.
The state of being left alone, free from observation of and interference with personal relations. privacy,
Property held by a private citizen or entity, not available for public or governmental use. property,
A charitable or educational organization run by a private group, not by the government.
Offering securities for sale to private individuals but not to the public.
The work done by a professional such as a doctor or lawyer who works for his or her own profit and not for the benefit of the government or the public.
The part of the economy not controlled by the government, in which private citizens work for their own profit.

(1) A right, immunity, or advantage held by only one or a few people, or only by a particular group or class. (2) An exemption from duties or requirements imposed on most people; a release from an obligation or liability.

A communication that does not have to be revealed during discovery because it occurred in a special situation, such as information revealed to a doctor by a patient or from one spouse to another. See also attorney-client privilege, patient-physician privilege, marital communications privilege, priest-penitent privilege.

A phrase used in the Constitution to refer to rights and protections held by all U.S. citizens by virtue of their citizenship, such as the right to travel from state to state and to be accorded the same rights granted to citizens of those states, the right to vote in federal elections, and the right to do business in various states.

A relationship between parties that occurs when they share an interest in or right to some property or matter, such as two people who enter a contract, or a deceased person and his or her heir, or two people who own the same piece of property one after the other.
A person who shares privity with another; a person with an interest in an action or property. ADJ. Sharing in private information.
(Latin) For the sake of good; describes work done without compensation for the public good. ADV. pro bono.

(Latin) For the sake of form; as a matter of form, rather than based on facts; for politeness. (1) Describes judgments rendered to facilitate further proceedings rather than on the basis of facts. (2) Describes accounting statements showing anticipated income before it actually occurs. ADV. pro forma.
(Latin) According to the rate;
(Latin) For oneself; appearing on one's own behalf; describes a person who represents himself or herself in a lawsuit.
Pro tempore.
(Latin) For the time being; temporary or provisional;

Reason to believe that a particular fact is more likely true than not; when considering doing search and seizure, reasonable grounds supported by evidence to assume that the search or arrest is justified, i.e., that a crime has been committed or that the property to be seized is associated with a crime.
A formal court procedure in which a will is proven to be valid or invalid; the entire process of settling the estate of a deceased person. V. To establish that a will is valid.
A court that handles wills, estates, and the appointment of guardians for orphaned children.

A sentencing procedure in which, instead of imprisoning a person convicted of a crime, the court releases him or her to the supervision of a probation officer, with the understanding that a violation of the terms of probation will result in a prison sentence.
A specified time during which an employer may assess the skills and suitability of a new hire and may choose to terminate the employee if he or she is deemed not suitable.
Proving something.
Evidence that proves what it is intended to prove.
The quality or level of proof offered by a particular piece of evidence.
The guarantee of a fair civil procedure that follows the rules, including the right to be notified about a complaint or charge and the right to be heard in court.

A method of doing something; the formal steps and methods used in conducting a lawsuit. See also civil procedure, criminal procedure, Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure.
A lawsuit or legal action; a step or event that is part of a lawsuit; a hearing, inquest, investigation, or other action that takes place before a judicial officer.
Money earned from some enterprise or activity.
(1) A series of actions taken to achieve some result; a normal method or procedure. (2) A summons, writ, warrant, or
A person who is legally authorized to deliver process documents to a defendant, such as a sheriff. See also service of process.
To announce or publish; to make an official or public declaration.

The act of announcing something officially or publicly; an official or public announcement, especially of some governmental action or policy; a bailiff's announcement that the court is about to do something.
An agent or proxy; a person who manages another person's affairs.
Appointing an attorney or agent to act on one's behalf; acting as a proxy or agent for someone.
A person who acts on someone else's behalf.
(1) To obtain something; to obtain a prostitute for someone. (2) To persuade someone to do something. N. procurement.
A person who procures; a pimp.
(1) To make, manufacture, or create. N. product. (2) To present for viewing or examination; to present a witness or exhibit to the court.
A doctrine of tort law that holds manufacturers and sellers liable for injuries caused by defective products they introduce into the marketplace.

(1) An occupation or job that requires extensive formal training and usually certification by some body of professionals within the field, such as with law or medicine. (2) A declaration of belief; a public declaration or announcement. V. profess.

(1) A group of people in the same profession who work together in an organization similar to a corporation, also called a professional corporation. (2) A group of professionals who organize themselves to advance education, lobbying, or other interests held by all of them. ABBRV. p.a.
To offer; to offer or hold out something as proof or evidence.
Financial gain; income greater than the costs and expenses involved in earning it. V. To earn a profit; to benefit.
To prevent; to forbid by law or rule. N. prohibition.

A binding declaration that a person will do a certain act, which gives the person receiving the promise the right to expect performance. V. To declare or assure someone that one will definitely do some act.
A person who receives a promise.
A person who promises something.
Implying or containing a promise.

A condition that arises when someone promises something to another person who relies on that promise and takes action that would hurt him or her if the promise is not kept; a court will find that such a promise is binding if there is no other way to avoid injustice to the injured party.
A document in which the maker promises to pay a certain sum of money on a specified date.
(1) To encourage or support. (2) To raise someone to a higher position or rank. (3) To form a corporation and raise capital for it. N. promotion.
A person who founds a corporation.
(1) To make widely known; to publish. (2) To put a statute into effect; to announce a statute or rule officially. N. promulgation.
The use of evidence or argument to establish a fact; evidence offered at trial to show the truth of some proposition.
Correct; appropriate; suitable.
The degree of care an ordinary person of ordinary prudence would use in a given situation.
The degree of attention, care, and prudence an ordinary person of ordinary prudence driving a car would use while driving under similar conditions.
A party with an interest in a lawsuit who could be joined and whose presence would help the just settlement of the matter, but without whom the lawsuit could still continue.
That which is or can be owned, possessed, used, and disposed of; those things owned by someone; possessions, land, buildings, and all rights and interests that can be owned. property,
The disposition of marital property between spouses after a divorce.
A tax levied by the government on the value of property owned by taxpayers.
pro rata.
System of election that distributes seats in proportion to the number of votes cast for particular candidates or parties.
To suggest or offer an idea for consideration. N. proposal.
Related to ownership; acting like an owner; owned by a particular person or company;
A function or duty that a municipal government performs on behalf of its citizens.
Trade secret information in which a person has an ownership interest.
A trade name or trademark; a registered name of a product that may not be used without the owner's permission.
Rights over property that an owner has by virtue of ownership.
A person who owns a business; a person with legal rights or title to a business.
A business owned by one person, usually not incorporated; synonymous with sole proprietorship.
To distribute proportionately.
To discontinue or suspend a session, as of a legislature, without dissolving it; to end a legislative session; to postpone.
To forbid an action; to denounce, outlaw, or condemn a person. N. proscription.
(1) To begin legal proceedings against someone, especially by the state against an accused criminal. (2) To continue to carry out some action, intending to complete it.
The act of prosecuting; the party prosecuting a case; a criminal action.
An attorney who prosecutes criminal cases on behalf of the state.
Future; expected to happen in the future.
A law scheduled to be enacted at some future date and that will apply only to cases arising after it is enacted.
A document issued by a corporation disclosing its financial information to current and prospective investors.
A person who engages in sex acts for money.
The practice of selling sex for money. See also pander, pimp.
To keep someone or something safe; to keep intact; to preserve something that exists. N.
protective.
The policy of limiting trade with foreign nations in an attempt to protect domestic producers.
A fee imposed on imported goods of a certain type to protect producers and sellers of domestic goods of the same type.
The confinement of a person to protect him or her from harm, either self-inflicted or inflicted by others.
An order issued by a court to protect a party from the abusive discovery, service of process, or other aspect of the legal process.
A duty or tariff on imported goods intended to discourage imports and protect domestically manufactured goods.

(1) A formal statement objecting to something; a statement of disapproval or dissent, often made to avoid implying consent by silence. (2) A written declaration witnessed by a notary that a bill or note has been presented for payment and refused. V. protest.

(1) Proper procedure, behavior, and etiquette at a diplomatic occasion or government function; the procedural rules for an organization, government, or situation. (2) A draft of a treaty or other diplomatic document; the minutes to a diplomatic meeting, signed by participants. (3) An addition to a treaty or international convention.
To establish the truth of something by using evidence and arguments. See also proof.
To stipulate a condition in a legal document.
A requirement or condition stated in a legal document.
Temporary; arranged as a temporary measure to keep affairs in order until a permanent solution can be arranged.
A temporary government created to keep order until a permanent government can be installed.
A temporary remedy given to a plaintiff to meet a present need while a lawsuit is in progress.
A condition or stipulation added to an agreement or contract.
To cause someone to act, usually by deliberately annoying or irritating him or her. N. provocation.
Closest in relationship, time, physical distance, or causal connection; immediate; direct. See also cause.
A substitute, deputy, or agent; a person authorized to act on behalf of someone else, especially someone allowed to vote for stockholders in a corporate election.
Cautious; careful;

A rule guiding investment by trustees in some states, limiting a trustee's investments to securities that a prudent person of reasonable intelligence and discretion would choose to provide a decent income and preserve capital.
Showing excessive fascination with nudity, sexual matters, or excrement.
An excessive interest in sex, nudity, wanton behavior, lasciviousness, etc.
Altering brain chemistry to affect mood, perception, and behavior. Synonymous with psychotropic.
See psychoactive.
(1) Concerning all people or an entire community. (2) Shared; common to or belonging to all the members of a community. (3) Done or provided by the government; involved in the government. (4) Open;
An attorney employed by the government to represent criminal defendants who cannot afford to pay for a lawyer.

The body of knowledge and creative works, including works of film, music, literature, and inventions, whose copyrights are not owned by anyone and are thus considered available for free use by anyone.
A person who is famous or well-known in a community or in society in general, such as a famous athlete, actor, or politician.
The well-being of the entire community.
An interest or concern important to an entire community or all of society.
A position that exists to do some job necessary to and on behalf of the government, held by a public official who is elected by the people and exercises some sovereign powers of the government.

The principles that guide a government in its administration, in an effort to maintain the well-being and order of the state and its citizens; also, the principle that a contract or transaction that harms the public good is illegal.
Property owned by the citizenry or the government as opposed to a private individual.
The intention or objective of benefiting or serving the public; used to describe government actions done to benefit the community directly; also called governmental purpose.
Documents filed in government offices that are open to the public for examination, such as records of real estate transactions.
The part of the economy controlled by the government, not by private individuals working for profit. See also private sector.
Work done by a corporation that serves the needs of the public, such as transportation or utilities.
A privately owned corporation that provides a public service, usually with a franchise from the state.
Benefit to the entire community brought about through the use of property condemned by the government through eminent domain.

A company that supplies a service, such as electricity, water, or gas, that is so essential to the public that the government will allow it to use public property and to function as a monopoly in exchange for government regulation of price and the requirement that it serve all customers without discrimination.
Making something public; preparing and issuing a book, article, or other document for sale to the public. V. publish.

(1) To prepare a book or document and issue it for sale to the public. (2) To make information widely known, such as by advertising in a newspaper. (3) To inform witnesses that one is signing a document as one's will. N. publication.

To exaggerate the quality of goods in advertising or in describing them to prospective buyers, intending to make them look good and improve the chances of someone purchasing them but not actually misleading the customer because the puffing is done as an expression of opinion, not a statement of fact.
To inflict a penalty on someone as retribution for a crime or misdeed. N. punishment.
Intended as punishment; related to punishment.
To buy; to acquire something by exchanging money or other valuable consideration for it. N. purchase.
A person who buys something.
A person who gives valuable consideration in exchange for the item purchased.
To steal.
Unbiased, not influenced by outside sources.
Intentionally; done with conscious intent and awareness of likely consequences.
To chase; to try to accomplish something; to engage in some activity. N. pursuit.

An inalienable right listed in the Declaration of Independence; includes freedom to pursue an occupation of one's choice, the right to have a family, freedom from discrimination or oppression, freedom of contract, and other personal freedoms necessary to create a life of one's own choosing and to one's own satisfaction.
Within the scope of something, such as a statute; the enacting portion of a statute. puff 402
Reputed; generally assumed to be; supposed.
A man who is assumed to be the father of a child born out of wedlock.
A person who believes he or she is legally married to someone although the marriage is actually invalid.

A sales scheme, often illegal, in which a buyer of goods is promised a commission for every additional buyer he or she finds and recruits to be a seller, and in which the people at the top of the pyramid collect payments from all the bottom tiers but the people at the bottom usually lose money. See also Securities and Exchange Commission. 403 pyramid scheme
The use of paper profits from existing investments to buy more investments.
qualified.
A written statement made by a taxpayer refusing to accept an interest in a particular property.
Expenses that meet certain criteria, such as specific medical expenses.

(1) To be eligible for or entitled to something by meeting a required condition. (2) To meet the criteria for becoming a member of a profession or to hold an office. (3) To restrict the meaning of something; to add reservations to a statement. N.

(1) The standard of excellence of an item as compared to other similar items; the comparative rank of a person or thing; excellence. (2) A characteristic; a unique attribute that can be used to distinguish one thing or person from another.

(Latin) As much as one deserves; used to describe a reasonable sum to pay to a person for some service if the price is not specified in a contract; used to prevent the unjust enrichment of someone who has received services under circumstances that should have notified him or her that the plaintiff providing the service expected to be paid.
A period of time, condition, or place of isolation for someone who has been exposed to an infectious disease, intended to prevent the disease from spreading into the community. V. quarantine.
To reject, suppress, or end; to make legally invalid.
(Latin) Almost, as if; apparently but not actually; seemingly.
An obligation similar to a contract imposed by the law when two parties have made no promises to one another but when one party has benefited from services provided by
Jurisdiction based on a defendant's interest in property within the court's jurisdiction, even when the defendant does not actually live there.
Describes the actions and powers of administrative officers and agencies that can perform some judicial tasks, although they are not in fact judges or courts.
A question. V. To ask a question.

(1) A sentence worded in such a way as to search for and discover information; an interrogatory statement to a witness asking him or her for information. (2) A point that is not settled and is open to debate; a doubt about some matter. V. To ask someone for information; to interrogate. See also hypothetical; matter of fact, matter of law.

(Latin) Who also. Qui tam pro domino rege quam pro si ipso in hac parte sequitur; someone who sues in this case on behalf of the ruling king as much as if on his own behalf; used to name a legal action brought by an individual plaintiff on behalf of the state or government as well as on the plaintiff's own behalf, such as a case brought by a whistleblower.
The point at which a pregnant woman feels the fetus move inside her womb.
(Latin) Something for something; giving one valuable thing in exchange for something else.
Undisturbed use of land or buildings that one is leasing.
To determine title to land in court by bringing all interested parties into court and allowing them to attempt to establish their claims; this court proceeding is called an action to quiet title.
(1) To leave a place; to give up possession of property. (2) To stop some activity; to resign from a job. ADJ. Rid of or free from something.
The act of giving up a claim on someone else or relinquishing a title to something. V. quitclaim.
A deed in which a person transfers whatever title he or she has to property, without guaranteeing that he or she has any title at all.
(Latin) With what intent.
The minimum number of members or delegates that must be present at a meeting of a body to validate any decisions made or business transacted at that meeting.
A goal; a fixed number of objects or people that are allowed or desired someplace; a proportional share of something.

(1) To copy or read words verbatim from a source, such as a statute or document. (2) To state a price for something or the cost of doing a job; to state an official price for the sale of securities. N. quotation.
(Latin) Quod vide; which see; used to direct a reader to another part of a document for more information about a topic. quit 408
(1) An ethnic group. (2) An election.

A recording act that allows the first person to record a deed to take precedence over others who record deeds to the same property subsequently, even if the first person to record knows of other claims to the land.

A recording act that allows the first person to record a deed to take precedence over those who record deeds to the same property subsequently, unless the first person to record knows of other unrecorded claims to the land.
Related to ethnicity or race; because of race.
The use by law enforcement officers of racial quotas instead of actual grounds for suspicion in their selection of people to stop for questioning.
Prejudice against members of a particular ethnic group or race.

term: Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act
definition: A federal law designed to fight organized crime. ABBRV. RICO.

Engaging in a conspiracy to commit business fraud, extortion, or coercion; using threats to extort money, goods, or services from business owners. See also mafia, organized crime, Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act.

A device that uses electromagnetic waves to track the position, speed, and direction of motion of a vehicle, commonly used by police officers to detect people driving automobiles faster than the legal speed limit.
Politically extreme, most often extremely liberal; supporting extreme change or complete reform of a system of government. N. radical. See also revolution.

(1) To increase; to make higher; to increase the face amount on a check, usually by forgery. (2) To suggest; to bring up a topic for consideration; to introduce an issue to the court, as through pleadings. (3) To collect money, as by asking for donations; to gather taxes. (4) To care for a child until adulthood. N. An increase in salary or compensation.
A sum of money or other valuable item demanded for the release of a prisoner,
The crime of having sexual intercourse with someone without his or her consent and against his or her will;
Able to be estimated; proportional; able to be measured according to some system of proportions.
An estate with a value that can be appraised, usually for taxes.
(1) A measure or value relative to some other measure or value; a proportion. (2) A price; a fee; a charge per unit of service or goods. V. To measure or assess according to a standard.
The percentage of profit made on an investment.
To approve, affirm, or confirm something, such as a law or contract; to make something valid. N. ratification.

A test applied by a court to determine whether a law challenged on constitutional grounds has a rational basis for existence, i.e., that it is intended to and does accomplish some legitimate governmental objective, that it is reasonable and not arbitrary, and that it treats all similar people the same way.
To rape; to have sexual intercourse by force and against the victim's will. N. ravishment.
The act of resuming possession of property that one possessed formerly.
Extremely conservative; opposed to social and political reform;
An amount that is owed to a business; an account that records amounts due.
Land, buildings, and other immovable objects fixed to the ground.
Land and the buildings attached to it; see also property, real.
An investment company that invests in real estate, distributes income to shareholders, and is given special income tax treatment. ABBRV. REIT.
Evidence consisting of actual things that can be seen and inspected by the jury at trial; see also demonstrative evidence.
A person with the legal right to enforce a claim and to benefit from a lawsuit if successful. real property. See property, real.
To make money from something; to receive profits from investments. N. realization.
Actual and identifiable gain or loss received by selling property or an investment. See also recognize.
Real estate; real property.

To change the way something is distributed; to change the boundaries of legislative districts or to change the number of representatives per district in order to keep representation proportional to population. N. reapportionment. See also gerrymander.

Presentation to the court of more oral arguments on an issue it has already heard, in cases where it seems that an important point has been overlooked or where there might be some confusion about facts.

(1) A cause, motive, or explanation for something. (2) Common sense; mental ability to think, distinguish between right and wrong, and to discern and interpret facts. V. To think about facts and form a judgment based on them; to attempt to persuade someone to think a certain way through the use of evidence and logic.
Appropriate; based on common sense and good judgment; fair and just.
A belief based on facts, circumstances, and information trustworthy enough to make a person of ordinary intelligence and caution believe something is the case, commonly used
The care that a person of ordinary prudence and competence would use in a similar situation. See also care, ordinary.
Enough uncertainty that a person has committed a crime, based on evidence or lack thereof, that a person of ordinary prudence and logical ability would hesitate to find him or her guilty.

A hypothetical person with ordinary intelligence, prudence, caution, and good judgment who is interested in protecting his or her own interests and the safety and well-being of others, used as a benchmark for measuring the behavior of a defendant in a negligence case; also called a reasonable man or reasonable woman in cases where gender is a factor.
A fair and appropriate amount of time to do something under given circumstances.
To respond to an argument or claim with contrary arguments and evidence; to refute.
A presumption that can be overturned by contrary evidence, but that becomes conclusive if contrary evidence is not presented.
A presumption that can be refuted with evidence; an assumption that is held to be true until refuted by evidence.

The opportunity given to a defending party to respond to and refute the arguments presented by the party presenting a case in chief, or to the party who initiates a closing argument after the other party has responded to that argument; an oral argument responding to and refuting an initial case.
Evidence offered to refute or combat claims made and evidence presented by an opposing party.

(1) To revoke; to reverse or cancel a judgment. (2) To remove an elected official from office before the end of his or her term. (3) To order an official, particularly a diplomatic official, to come back home and often to relinquish his or her office. (4) To notify consumers of a manufacturing defect in a product and allow them to return the product for repair or replacement. N. recall.
To reorganize the capital structure of a corporation; to rearrange the stocks, bonds, and other securities of a corporation,
(1) To collect tax that was previously claimed by a taxpayer as a deduction or credit. (2) To take back something that has been captured by an enemy. N. recapture.

A person or business appointed by a court to manage property owned by an insolvent person or business that is the subject of a lawsuit, to hold the property and preserve it for the benefit of those who will ultimately receive it.

The state of an insolvent business or person whose property has been put under the care of a receiver for management, preservation, and distribution at the end of a lawsuit; the equitable proceeding in which a receiver is appointed.
The crime of receiving and accepting property or goods known to be stolen; see also fence.
A break in a court or legislative session in which official proceedings are suspended for a short time.
The practice of committing crimes regularly.
A repeat offender; a person who habitually and regularly commits crimes. ADJ. recidivist.
Mutual; affecting both parties equally; done in return for something.
A bilateral contract; a contract in which each party does something for the other.
A law that extends rights and privileges to citizens of another state in exchange for the other state extending its rights and privileges to the first state.
An easement that automatically arises when an owner of two or more related properties sells one of them with a restriction on it, binding the other properties with the same restrictions.

An agreement or relationship between states, countries, or people, in which the rights and privileges of one are observed in the other; e.g., an attorney who is a member of a bar in one state is allowed to practice law in states that have reciprocity with the first state.
(Archaic) To pay attention to something.
Without regard to the consequence of one's actions; careless;
Wanton or careless inattention to the safety of others or to consequences while conscious of risks, but without intent to cause harm.
Driving a vehicle without attention to risks, consequences, or the safety of others.
A promise or obligation to do an act required by law, such as to appear in court on a particular date to answer criminal charges.
(1) To include money earned in taxable income. (2) To acknowledge; to confirm. N. recognition. See also realize.
To remember; to recall to mind. N. recollection.
To settle a disagreement; to restore friendly relations between people, states, or nations; to correct discrepancies in a document or account. N. reconciliation.

An official written report about some event or transaction; written documents, audio and video tapes, and other documentary information. V. To write an account of some event or transaction to serve as an official version of what happened.
A date by which a shareholder must be registered with a corporation in order to receive dividends and vote in elections.
The public acknowledgement of a person's claim to property that occurs when the owner publicly records an instrument of conveyance, such as a deed.
The documents that constitute the history of a lawsuit, including pleadings, evidence, motions, briefs, and other papers filed with the court.
The person who is registered as the official owner of company stock. See also public record.

A state statute that regulates the recording of real estate documents such as deeds, mortgages, and leases, with particular attention to those cases in which more than one person claims the same property.
A trade organization that lobbies on behalf of recorded music producers, particularly in the area of copyright and file sharing. ABBRV. RIAA.
To recover; to reclaim something that had been lost; for a defendant to have a plaintiff's award for damages reduced through some claim the defendant has on the plaintiff. N. recoupment.
Assistance in difficulty; a source of help; a right to demand compensation.
A loan guaranteed by a guarantor or other endorser who agrees to pay the lender if the borrower defaults.
(1) To return to a normal condition, as of a person's health or the economy. (2) To regain control or possession of something. (3) To receive compensation as a result of a lawsuit.
(1) A return to normal condition. (2) The act of recovering something; the restoration of a right or possession. (3) An amount awarded to the prevailing party in a lawsuit.
An accusation made by an accused person against his or her accuser, especially in a divorce case.
To correct; to make something right. N. rectification.

For a judge to withdraw from hearing a lawsuit because of self-interest, bias, or other inability to render a fair and impartial decision; to object to a judge or jury on the grounds that he or she will not be impartial. N. recusal.
A group of humanitarian organizations in several countries that work to help people in need, such as disaster victims.

(1) A misleading clue; a point in a case that might seem important but that is in fact irrelevant to the main issue. (2) In securities, a preliminary prospectus about a future stock issue not yet approved by the Securities and Exchange Commission, so-called because it is bordered in red to alert investors that it is not yet approved.
Excessive bureaucracy; the excessive requirements, paperwork, and technicalities that are part of doing business with a bureaucracy.
To buy something back; to exchange a coupon or negotiable instrument for money; to clear a debt, such as a mortgage; for a corporation to buy back its own stock. N. redemption.
The act of redeeming;
To refuse to give someone a mortgage loan based on the neighborhood in which the prospective borrower lives; to refuse to insure a dwelling based on its neighborhood. N. redlining.
(Latin) Reduction to the point of absurdity; a method of arguing against a proposition by showing that its ultimate conclusion is absurd.
The inclusion of words or arguments that could be omitted without harming the ultimate meaning; the inclusion of unnecessary material in a document or pleading.
Unnecessary; superfluous.
(1) To direct attention or allude to something. (2) To send a matter to some other body; to send a case to a referee for investigation; to submit a contract to arbitration. N. reference.
An officer appointed by a court to hear testimony from the parties to a case and report his or her findings to the court.

(1) A citation; a mention of the source of information; a book or other document used as a source of information. (2) A person who provides information about another person, usually to a prospective employer; a letter from a person providing information about another person. (3) The act of referring a matter to another body. V. refer.
A state or local election in which the voters vote on an item of legislation or an amendment.
To replace an existing loan with a new one, usually at a lower interest rate; in securities, to issue new bonds to raise money for the payment of bonds about to mature.
To correct; to change something to make it better; to change oneself or another person for the better; to correct a written instrument under court order.
A court-ordered rewriting of a contract or other instrument in cases where the instrument does not say what the parties intended it to say.
An institution where juvenile criminals are sent instead of prison, with an emphasis on education and improved behavior; also called a reform school.
An individual who has been forced to flee his or her home due to war, fear of persecution, or natural disaster. See also asylum.

A return of money paid to some person or institution, such as to a dissatisfied customer or to a taxpayer who has paid too much in taxes. V. (1) To return money already paid, such as to a customer or taxpayer. (2) To refinance; to fund a debt again; to sell new bonds to pay off a loan.
Rejected things; things that have been thrown away, such as garbage and trash. V. To decline to do something. N. refusal.
severally; see also joint.
To enroll; to enter something in an official list or record. N.
An officer responsible for recording property transactions in the public records.

Trust or confidence that someone will do something or that something will happen; a belief that something will happen that causes the believer to do something he or she would not have done otherwise. V. rely.
(1) Assistance, especially money or food given to those in need; an act or benefit that alleviates some hardship or distress. (2) A replacement for someone who has been on duty. V. relieve.
Something that is left over; a part of an estate in land left over after the rest of the estate has been settled; a future interest in an estate.
A person entitled to a future interest in an estate in land; a person who will take the remainder of an estate after all testamentary bequests are settled.
(1) For an appellate court to send a case back to a lower court for reconsideration. (2) To place someone in custody, such as a defendant,
Intended to cure or to act as a remedy.

The process of restoring good title to property to a person who formerly had good title but currently does not.
The reduction of a jury's excessive verdict or award of damages.

For a lender to take possession of an item or property if the person who borrowed money to purchase it fails to make payments. N. repossession. See also foreclose.

To revoke or cancel a contract or agreement. N. rescission.
The revocation or cancellation of a contract or agreement; a cancellation of a contract that returns the parties to their condition before they entered into it. V. rescind.

A written court order providing directions on how to dispose of a case; a short judicial opinion by an appellate court providing reasons for its decision to be sent to the trial court that originally heard the case in question.
To save someone or something from danger or destruction. N. rescue.

Entitlement to the first opportunity to do something before the opportunity is offered to others.

Money that is accumulated and held for the future.
A bank account in which money is held and accumulated, as opposed to being spent regularly as in a checking account.

Words used to describe a conveyance that has been properly executed, signed, witnessed, sealed, and delivered to the proper recipient.

Coming after something; less important than something else; subsequent.

Not known by others;

Money or property given to a creditor by a debtor as a pledge of payment, usually returned to its owner if payment is completed satisfactorily; e.g., money given to a landlord by a tenant as a guarantee that the tenant will pay rent and observe other terms of the lease, to be forfeited if the tenant damages the property or otherwise breaks the bargain.
An interest in real or personal property that exists because the property has been offered as security for an obligation or debt.
Speech or actions intended to persuade the people to rise up against a government and, if possible, overthrow it;
(1) To tempt or entice someone into sexual activity. (2) To tempt someone into doing something he or she knows is wrong or ill-advised. N. seduction.

An agency of the executive branch of the U.S. government that administers the draft, requiring all males between the ages of 18 and 26 to register and inducting them into the armed services when necessary.
A fiduciary's use of property or funds entrusted to him or her for personal benefit, such as a trustee's using trust property for himself or herself.

The act of testifying against oneself or implicating oneself in a crime, which the Fifth Amendment forbids the government to require of anyone.
To give someone property in exchange for money; to be in the business of selling something. N. sale.

A person who sells something.

The upper chamber of the U.S. Congress; the upper chamber of a state legislature; a governing body of a nation, university, or other organization.
A member of a senate.
The condition of having been in a job longer than someone else, which can result in preference for promotions and layoffs.
The punishment given by the court to a criminal defendant who has been found guilty of a crime. V. sentence. sentence,
Simplified Employee Pension Individual Retirement Account.

A cause of action brought as part of a larger suit that could be separated and could stand alone as its own lawsuit, which can serve as a basis for removing the entire lawsuit from state to federal court.
Not connected with something; distinct;
Describing a doctrine, now generally believed to be unconstitutional, that held that the races could be treated equally although segregated into different facilities.

A written agreement between a husband and wife who have separated and intend to divorce, arranging for division of property, custody of children, alimony, child support, and any other necessary matters.

The division of the U.S. government and many state governments into three branches-executive, legislative, and judicial-each of which wields a particular set of powers unique to it and not shared by the other branches, and that the other branches are not permitted to use.

(1) To isolate; to separate or segregate; to hide away; to isolate a jury during a trial. (2) To seize property pending the outcome of litigation or to hold until a debt is paid; to impose spending restrictions on a government; to declare someone bankrupt. Also called sequestrate. N. sequestration.
The murder by one killer of several victims over a period of time.
A person who works for someone else and is controlled by him or her; an employee; a person employed by a government, also called a civil servant. See also civil service, independent contractor.
(1) To work for someone, especially as an employee; to hold a position in the armed forces. (2) To deliver a legal document such as a summons formally and in an official capacity.
The act of working for someone; the work that is performed by someone. service,
Delivery of a legal document through mail.
Publication of a summons or other document as an advertisement in a newspaper named by the court, used in cases where the defendant cannot be found.
The formal delivery to a defendant of the complaint, summons, or other legal document to notify him or her that a lawsuit has been brought.
A member of the military; a soldier in the armed forces.
Land subject to an easement; land that the owner of a dominant estate is allowed to use for some specific purpose.

(1) The condition of being enslaved; the condition of a person who is bound to work for another person as a servant, either voluntarily or involuntarily; see also indenture. (2) An obligation or encumbrance on land that takes the form of a burden or restriction on the landowner, as opposed to an easement, which is a right or privilege enjoyed by the owner of the estate that claims it. servitude,
A meeting of a court, legislature, or other body; the period when a court sits to hear cases.
