A bound volume of the laws passed during a legislative session, compiled in the order of their enactment.
To vacate, annul, or reverse a judgment, order, or proceedings.

A counterclaim brought by a defendant against a plaintiff that arises out of a different matter from that raised in the plaintiff's complaint, and that the defendant hopes to use to recover some amount from the plaintiff, thereby reducing or canceling the damages due to the plaintiff.
A building's distance from a curb, building, property line, or other boundary.

(1) To resolve a matter; to conclude an estate; to finalize accounts; for the parties to a lawsuit to resolve their dispute on their own before a court reaches a final judgment on the matter after trial, thereby allowing the trial to be canceled and the lawsuit terminated. (2) To dispose of finally, such as after death; to give property to someone. N. settlement.
A person who creates a trust; a person who gives property to someone; see also donor.
To cut; to divide or separate; to end a relationship; to separate causes of action or defendants that have been joined together to try them instead in individual lawsuits. N. severance.
Able to have portions removed without destruction of the whole;
A contract that contains two or more independent parts, one or more of which can potentially be breached without invalidating the entire contract.
A statute that remains valid after portions of it are declared invalid and removed.
(1) Three or more but not many. (2) Separate;
Individual lawsuits brought against multiple defendants by the same plaintiff out of the same subject matter.
Liability for something held by every individual involved with some matter, which allows for a separate lawsuit to be brought against each party.
The ownership of property by one person, who does not share title with anyone else.
A sum of money paid to a terminated employee as compensation for firing him or her.
Extreme; very bad; strict or harsh.

(1) Sexual activity; sexual intercourse. (2) A physical category, either male or female, determined by reproductive organs; members of a group that have the same reproductive organs; the state of being a male or female; see also gender. ADJ. sexual.
A crime involving sex, either with a sexual motive or involving a sexual act, such as rape, sexual assault, or indecent exposure.
A person who has been convicted of a sex crime.
Employment discrimination that involves sexual demands and acts, which can involve perpetrators and victims of either sex.
Illegal sexual contact between a minor and an adult.

Forcible sexual contact inflicted on an unwilling victim; see also rape. sexual harassment. See harassment, sexual. shall. V. A modal verb used to create a future tense; as used in statutes and legal documents, generally has an imperative sense, meaning "must," though it can also be used in a permissive sense, meaning "may." In traditional English usage, "shall" was the future
usage_examples: though it can also be used in a permissive sense, meaning

definition: Something that is false; a fake; a pretense. ADJ. False; not what it is purported to be.
A pleading that appears genuine but is not based on any real issue of material fact, and is so clearly false that it does not raise a bona fide issue.
A transaction that appears to be genuine but in fact is intended to deceive its creditors or the government.
To partake of a portion of something along with others, or to give a portion of something to another. N. A portion; a part of a larger thing; a unit of stock.
To divide something into equal portions for each person or entity that is entitled to a share.
A person who owns shares of stock; synonymous with stockholder.
Software that a user may acquire and try free of charge, usually distributed with a request for voluntary payment. See also open source.
Islamic law, based on interpretation of the Quran and other sources of Muslim doctrine.
An officer elected by a county to keep the peace, enforce laws, serve process, execute judgments, and perform other such
A sale of property by a sheriff or court officer ordered by a court to satisfy a judgment, such as a foreclosure, often conducted as an auction.
A federal statute passed in 1890 to prevent monopolies and restrictions on free and open interstate and foreign commerce; see also antitrust, Clayton Act.

A law that prevents a court from requiring witnesses to reveal certain kinds of information, such as a law that prevents opposing counsel from questioning a victim of a sex crime about his or her past sexual behavior, or a law that allows journalists to refuse to disclose confidential information in a legal proceeding; see also privilege.
To move something; to change position or emphasis; to transfer a burden of proof from one party to another.
(1) A place of business where goods are sold. (2) A place where goods are manufactured; a factory, plant, or workshop.
A union official who is elected to represent his or her fellow workers in negotiations with management.
Stealing merchandise from a store.

(1) Covering a small distance or lasting a brief time; below the standard or required length. (2) Having sold borrowed shares or property in the expectation of being able to repurchase them at a lower price; see also sell short.
For a short time; current; due within a short period of time, usually within a year.
To illustrate; to prove; to exhibit or display; to produce evidence of something.
An order by the court made upon the motion of one party requiring the opposing party to appear before it and explain why the court should not take a particular action.

A face-to-face confrontation arranged by law enforcement officers between a suspect and a witness to or victim of a crime, usually done just after the crime has been committed and intended to allow the witness or victim to identify the person who has committed the crime.
prudence.
A contract or licensing agreement that is packaged inside a product and that cannot be read until the product is purchased and opened. See also browse-wrap agreement, click-wrap agreement.
Physically or mentally ill; can also include incapacity due to physical injury.
A leave that an employee is allowed to take from work when either the employee or a close family member is sick or injured, either with or without pay but without loss of seniority or benefits.
Compensation paid to an employee while on sick leave either temporarily or permanently, often as part of disability compensation.

A place at the side of a judge's bench where the judge and attorneys can discuss matters privately, in the presence of the court reporter who records their conversation, but without the jury, parties, witnesses, or spectators hearing what they say.
A draft of money payable when presented. draft,
A negotiable instrument that is payable on sight, i.e., when presented; a draft that is payable on demand.
To write one's name on a document to authenticate or execute it.
A party who signs his or her name to an agreement; a nation that signs on to an international treaty or other agreement.

The act of signing one's name; one's name written by one's own hand, or affixed in some other way such as printing, engraving, stamping, allowing someone else to write one's name, a mark used instead of a name, etc., as long as the person whose name appears on the document intends it to be placed there to make the document effective.
An official statement issued by a president when he or she signs a bill into law, explaining his or her reasons for signing and opinions about the new law.
Not speaking; knowing something but not saying anything or acting accordingly. N. silence.
An investor in a business who contributes capital and shares profits and losses but does no active management.
Almost the same; resembling; alike in some ways.
Sales of properties similar to the one in question, used to determine fair market value.
Pure; uncomplicated; used to describe a crime that is not aggravated or compounded. See also aggravate.

term: Simplified Employee Pension Individual Retirement Account
definition: An individual retirement account in which an individual may contribute a set percentage of pre-tax income. ABBRV. SEP-IRA.
To imitate; to assume an appearance; to fabricate or counterfeit. N. simulation.
An invented fact; a lie.
A judgment on a case of bad debt that appears valid but actually is meant to give unfair advantage to one of the parties or to perpetrate a fraud on third parties.

A law stating that in cases where distribution of property depends on the time that a person dies, and where two people die simultaneously or close enough together that it is impossible to determine who died first (such as when husband and wife perish together in an airplane crash), the law will presume that each died before the other and their property should be distributed accordingly.
(Latin) Without which not; something without which something else cannot happen; a requirement or necessity.
A fund used to accumulate money to replace equipment as it wears out, to repay debts as bonds mature, or for other long-term financial obligations.
To hold a session of a court, a legislature, or other body.
(Latin) Site, position; the location of a thing; the location of property for tax purposes; the place where a crime or other action takes place.
Making a false and defamatory oral statement about someone that can injure his or her reputation;
Criticizing a product in order to lower public opinion of it or to persuade a customer to purchase a different product.
Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation.
A person owned by and forced to work for another person. See also enslave.
The care a reasonable person exercises in unimportant matters, or the care used by a careless person in a similar situation.

An accident in which a victim slips on something, such as liquid on the floor or a banana peel, and is injured, which serves as the basis for a lawsuit against the owner of the premises; a similar kind of accident is a trip and fall. See also personal injury, plaintiff's attorney.
A business with a small number of employees, typically fewer than fifty or one hundred, or with low sales or profits.
A court that handles matters involving small amounts of money in an informal, inexpensive, and usually fairly quick manner.

A national program of social insurance in which employers and employees all contribute funds that are pooled and paid out to retired and disabled workers and to the surviving spouses of dead workers, administered by the Social Security Administration.
Oral or anal sexual intercourse, or sexual intercourse with an animal; definition varies by state.
Goods made of fabric, such as clothing or bedding, or textiles themselves; also called dry goods. See also consumer goods.
The programs a computer uses to perform particular tasks; applications. See also hardware.
An owner or proprietor.
A person who owns a business alone, without co-owners or partners.
See proprietorship.
Formal; observing the proper form or ceremony.
To make formal through an official ceremony; to perform a marriage in public before witnesses. N. solemnization.
(1) To try to get something from someone; to ask for something; to request or entreat; to encourage someone to commit a crime. (2) To offer one's services as a prostitute. N. solicitation.

(1) The head legal officer of a city, town, department, or other body. (2) In Britain, a lawyer who drafts wills, prepares conveyances, and handles all other legal matters aside from arguing cases in court, though in modern times solicitors do occasionally appear in court; see also barrister.
The attorney who ranks just below the attorney general of the United States and whose responsibilities include representing the U.S. government before the Supreme Court and other federal courts.
Imprisoning someone alone in an isolated cell, without contact with fellow prisoners or without any human contact at all, even from prison employees.
Able to pay debts;
To provide an account of an event, either oral or written, based on investigation or observation.
In good condition; without defects;

term: sound and disposing mind and memory
definition: Language often used in wills to mean the mental capacity necessary to create a will.
Healthy, without disease, injury, or physical impairment.
To aim at; to be connected with.
For a lawsuit to appear to be an action related to a contract, although it was brought as an action in tort.
For an action to be brought for damages only, not for recovery of property.
Sane and mentally capable, with unimpaired faculties.
Origin; the place or person from which something comes; the person or thing that starts something.
A ruler; a monarch or king; the supreme authority of a country. ADJ. sovereign.
A doctrine that prohibits lawsuits being brought against the government without its consent.
A state or nation that governs itself and is not subject to the authority of any other state or nation.
The power by which a government rules its state or nation.
Unsolicited emails sent to large numbers of email addresses in the hope of enticing some readers to respond.
(1) The head of a legislative body. (2) A person who is speaking.
The chief officer of the House of Representatives.
A jury's determination on a particular question of fact alleged in the pleadings. See also directed verdict.
An attorney who works for the attorney general on a particular matter.
A group of people with a common interest who attempt to influence legislators to pass laws favorable to them.
A court's ability to hear only a limited category of cases.
A law that affects only particular people and not the general public; a private law.
A use of property that is not allowed under zoning laws but is permitted as an exception in a particular case.
Money in the form of metal coins. specie, in. ADJ. (1) In coins. (2) A contract performed exactly according to stipulated terms.
Clearly stated or identified; precisely defined;
An equitable remedy in a case of breach of contract, in which the breaching party is required to perform the obligation under the contract. See also bequest, intent.
specification.

(1) To guess; to theorize or come to a conclusion about something without adequate evidence. (2) To engage in risky investments; to buy or sell stocks in the hopes that prices will rise or fall dramatically. N. speculation.

A clause in the Constitution that provides congresspeople with immunity from prosecution arising out of any speech or debate conducted during a legislative session, to ensure that legislators may speak freely without fear of reprisal.
A criminal trial conducted according to proper regulations and without unnecessary or arbitrary delays, a right guaranteed by the Sixth Amendment.
A person who spends a great deal of money quickly and without prudence; a person who wastes his or her estate through excessive spending, gambling, laziness, or other irresponsible behavior.
A trust set up to safeguard the funds of someone who would be expected to waste them by restricting the beneficiary's access to money and placing it out of the reach of creditors.
The actual intention of a law; the meaning it is meant to convey, arrived at through interpretation and possibly consideration of the intentions of those who wrote it; see also letter of the law.

(1) To break into separate parts; to divide between two or more parties or entities. (2) To divide a single cause of action into several parts, each of which becomes the basis for its own action. ADJ. split.
The act of destroying something; destruction of evidence that might prove unfavorable;
Done on a sudden impulse without prior planning.
A statement made immediately after some exciting occurrence by someone who saw it or participated in it, before he or she has the chance to think about it; see also res gestae.
A husband or wife; a person who is married to another. ADJ. spousal. spouse,

Software that is secretly installed on a computer without the owner's knowledge or consent in order to collect personal information, to use the computer to send spam, to hijack the Web browser, or to conduct other illegal activities.
A person who sets up residence on property that does not belong to him or her. V. squat.
Eliminating minority shareholders from a corporation by buying out minority shares or issuing many new shares that dilute the minority interest; also called a freeze-out.
To apply multiple insurance policies to one loss.

(1) An interest, such as in a business. (2) A wager; an amount of money or item of property placed by two people in the care of a third party who will give it to the party who becomes entitled to it based on the outcome of some event, such as the winner of a game or lawsuit.

(1) A person with an interest in a business or transaction. (2) A third party who holds property or money that is the subject of a contest between two other people, such as a lawsuit or a wager. See also interpleader.
To follow or watch a person with no legitimate purpose and in such a way that it alarms the person being followed. N. stalking.

A person who stalks another. stand. (1) To appear in court when summoned to a trial. (2) To be in a particular state or condition; to remain in the same condition; for a law to remain in force. N. A position taken in an argument or fight.
For a criminal defendant to refuse to plead when charged.
A norm; an established practice; a level, quality, or idea used as a benchmark for comparison. ADJ. Accepted; normal.
The degree of care that an ordinary person of reasonable prudence would use in a specific situation.
The level of proof that must be offered to prove a case, such as "beyond a reasonable doubt" in criminal cases or a "preponderance of the evidence" in civil cases.
(1) Position or status in a community; reputation. (2) Having a strong enough interest in a matter to be allowed to bring a lawsuit based on it; also called standing to sue. ADJ. Permanent; regular.
An order that is carried out as part of a regular routine, used to regulate procedures in a court or other body.
(Latin) Stand by things decided; the principle that courts in common law will follow previously decided cases as much as possible and avoid upsetting precedents.
A newly created company.
To say; to express something in words,

A claim brought by a private citizen against the government, protesting governmental intrusion into his or her affairs and violation of his or her civil rights, usually brought under the due process clause of the Fourteenth Amendment and the Civil Rights Act.
Laws passed by and enforced within a state, as opposed to federal law.
The mental condition at the time that some event occurs; a person's reasons for acting in a particular way.
The police force hired by a state to keep the peace and enforce laws within a state.
A written or verbal expression of some fact or belief; an assertion or allegation made by a witness. See also opening statement, prior inconsistent statement.
(1) Social standing or rank; official classification. (2) Condition or state.

A crime based on a person's character or condition rather than on actually committing a wrongful act, such as the condition of being a drug addict; laws defining status crimes are generally held to be unconstitutional.
The usual state of things at a particular time.
The normal state of things before a particular event occurred.
A report on the status of a business or endeavor as of a particular date or time.
A formal written law passed by a legislature.
A statute requiring that certain kinds of contracts be written in order to be enforced, common under English law and now codified in the Uniform Commercial Code.
A statute that limits the time in which a lawsuit may be brought for an injury or a crime, after which the party with a grievance loses the right to sue; see also laches.
Related to statutes; required by or governed by a statute.

Interpretation of a statute, often done by a judge when a statute is invoked during a lawsuit; see also construe, construction. statutory rape. An act of sexual intercourse that involves consenting partners but is legally considered rape because one or both of the partners were below the legal age of consent.
To stop; to put on hold. N. A judicial order that puts some action on hold temporarily.
A judicial order that prevents a judgment from being executed for a period of time.
To take something that belongs to someone else without the owner's consent, intending to keep it or use it and never return it. ADJ. stolen. See also larceny, theft.
Cautious and secretive movement that is meant to go unnoticed. ADJ. stealthy.
A familial relationship through marriage, not blood.
A natural child of one's spouse from a previous marriage or liaison.
A person who is not a child's natural parent but who is married to the child's natural mother or father.
A basis that is increased to a certain level at a particular date, usually in property transferred through inheritance.

To require or demand something as part of an agreement; for the parties or attorneys on opposing sides of a case to agree in writing on how to handle certain parts of the lawsuit in order to limit issues and speed up the proceedings. N. stipulation.
A certificate entitling its holder to buy a certain number of shares of stock for a specified price.
A document that indicates that its holder has an ownership interest in a corporation.
A place or organization where shares of stock and other securities are bought and sold.
A stock exchange; the entire marketplace of securities trading facilities; the economic concept of securities trading; also called the market.
The right to buy stock at a predetermined price, usually better than market price, during a specified period, offered as incentive or compensation to employees and managers of a company.

The splitting of single shares into two or more shares apiece, resulting in an increased total number of shares and in the shares held by individual shareholders. See also broker, preferred stock, common stock, Securities and Exchange Commission, security.
A person employed by a customer to buy and sell securities on the customer's behalf.
A person who owns shares of stock; synonymous with shareholder.
To refuse to answer questions or to give evasive answers in order to delay or block some action, particularly a political action.
To prevent someone from moving; to restrain someone's liberty. N. stop.
The action by a police officer of stopping a person suspected to be armed and dangerous and running his or her hands over the outside of the suspect's clothing to locate any concealed weapons.

The securities trading practice of placing a contract to buy (put option) and a contract to sell (call option) on the same commodity or stock, hoping to profit from a large change in price in a volatile market. See also hedge.
A method of depreciation in which the cost of an asset is deducted in equal amounts over its life. See also accelerated cost recovery system.
A lawsuit filed by a large corporation against a much smaller adversary in order to silence the adversary by swamping it with legal burdens and costs. ABBRV. SLAPP.
(1) An argument placed in a brief or opinion solely for the purpose of refuting it. (2) A third party who acts as a
Constantly delivered over a telecommunications network.
The name of a broker, rather than that of a customer; refers to securities that are held in a broker's name instead of in the name of the customer who owns them.
Rigid; adhering closely to rules without deviation, relaxation, or room for interpretation.

(1) Tort liability imposed regardless of actual fault on a person or company that sells a product for injuries due to defective or dangerous products; see also products liability. (2) Criminal liability imposed regardless of criminal intent on a person who commits a certain type of criminal act, such as a parking violation.

A test that courts apply to determine the constitutionality of statutes that classify people by type (such as race or national origin) in ways that affect fundamental rights, in which the government has the burden of proving that there is a compelling state interest served by the classification and that the classification is the only way of achieving that purpose.

A refusal to work by a group of employees, usually done to force management to grant some request made by the workers after negotiations have not produced the desired result; a refusal by a group of people to do some action that is expected of them as a way of forcing compliance with their demands. V. (1) To participate in a work stoppage. (2) To remove someone from a group; to eliminate a prospective juror.

(1) To remove a case from a court's docket due to lack of jurisdiction. (2) To finalize a sale at an auction by the auctioneer bringing down his or her hammer or otherwise announcing that the highest bidder is entitled to pay the amount of the bid and take the property.
A person brought in to do the work of a striking employee; also called a scab.
A trademark that is inherently distinctive, such as an arbitrary or fanciful one, which is entitled to greater protection than a weaker one, such as a generic or descriptive mark. trademark,
(Latin) Of one's own volition; voluntarily; without prompting.
(Latin) Under a judge; under consideration by a court.
A person or business hired by a general contractor to perform a portion of a contracted project. See also contractor.
To divide a part of something into smaller parts; to divide a plot of land into smaller parcels or lots. N. subdivision.
(1) A topic; a person or thing that is being considered or discussed. (2) A citizen; a resident of a state or nation,
A matter or topic presented for consideration or debate; the right or property that is the foundation of a dispute or lawsuit.
A court's power to hear cases related to a particular kind of subject matter.
To raise an issue for debate; to discuss.
A lease of property to a third person by a person who is already leasing it; the original lessee remains responsible for the original lease. V. sublet.
(1) To present something to someone else for consideration or decision; to present a proposal to the court for approval. (2) To yield to someone else's desires or authority; to obey.

Below someone or something else; of lower rank or importance. N. A person ranked below another. V. To rank something below something else; to give one debt or claim lower priority than another debt or claim. N. subordination.
To persuade someone to commit perjury through bribery or other inducement. N. subornation.
An official court document ordering a person to appear in court or at a judicial proceeding at a specified time; also called subpoena ad testificandum.
A subpoena commanding a witness to bring certain documents and other relevant items to court or to a deposition.

A loan made to an individual who cannot obtain a loan from a traditional lender at market rates due to a low credit rating, typically made at a higher interest rate to offset the risks incurred by the lender.

The substitution of one person for another with respect to an obligation, legal claim, or debt, with the substituted person assuming all duties of payment or performance and all rights that accompany the claim. V. subrogate.
A person who is substituted for another.
A person who substitutes another person for himself or herself. sub rosa. (Latin) Under the rose; secret or confidential.

(1) To write one's name on a document as a means of authenticating it or making it binding upon him or her; see also sign. (2) To agree to buy shares in a corporation. (3) To give money to a cause, such as a charity. (4) To pay a sum of money in order to receive something regularly, such as issues of a magazine. N. subscription.
A person who subscribes.
The right of a person who already holds shares in a corporation to buy at a favorable price additional shares of the same type of stock.
A grant of money from the government or a public body to assist an industry that provides services that are needed by the community. V. subsidize.
Using drugs or alcohol to the point that they detrimentally affect the life of the user and those around him or her.
Important; large; considerable; valuable.
Evidence adequate to support the proposition for which it is offered.
Performance that does not exactly meet the terms of an agreement but that does accomplish the
To provide evidence to support a contention or prove a fact; to verify; to back up a story.
(1) Related to actual rights or duties rather than rules or procedures. (2) Based in reality; having some real meaning.
The requirement found in the Fifth and Fourteenth Amendments that the substance and content of laws must be fair and reasonable and that they must not be applied arbitrarily or unreasonably.
Evidence offered to prove a fact at issue in a trial, as opposed to character evidence about a witness.

The part of law that defines rights and duties rather than procedural methods of enforcing those rights, i.e., the law of contract, tort, property, trusts and estates, etc., as opposed to civil procedure.
To replace one thing or person with another. N. A replacement.
Service in which the document is presented to someone authorized to receive it on the named party's behalf.
A person who leases property from someone who is already leasing himself or herself; a person who enters into a sublease.
A ploy to escape, evade, or conceal something; a deceitful means of accomplishing some goal.
subversive.
To work to undermine established authority or overthrow the government. N.
(1) To achieve a desired goal. N.
successful. (2) To take the place of someone or something in a

(1) Several people or things of the same type following one after the other. (2) Inheriting a title or right to property through a will. (3) Following someone in a position, office, or other situation. See also succeed.
A person or thing that takes the place of another in a position, elected office, or other situation.
The next person who would have title to or control over a property if the current owner were to lose it. See also succeed.
To initiate and carry out legal proceedings against someone; to bring a lawsuit against someone. (2) To appeal to someone to grant something. N. suit.
(1) To experience pain or misery. (2) To tolerate.
Tolerance; consent to some action implied by failing to object rather than explicitly approving.
Pain and misery.
Adequate; enough for the purpose for which it is required. N. sufficiency.
Enough evidence to justify the legal action in question.
The right to vote in a public election. suffrage,
A name for a product that evokes characteristics of the product, and that is considered inherently distinctive and thus allowed to be registered.
(Latin) Of its own kind; unique; the only example of its type.
(Latin) Of one's own right; emancipated; of legal age; having the right to handle one's own affairs and not under the legal control of anyone else.
The act of killing oneself. V. commit suicide.
(1) A lawsuit; a legal action in which a plaintiff appeals to a court to grant him or her a remedy for some injury. (2) An entreaty or appeal to an authority to grant some request.
(1) A person who applies or appeals to do something, such as a man who hopes to persuade a woman to marry him or a person who hopes to buy a business. (2) A person who brings a lawsuit; a plaintiff.
A specified or fixed sum of money.
(1) Concise. (2) Conducted without the usual formalities. (3) Done by a judge or magistrate without a jury.
A concise statement of the main points of a longer document. V.
A judgment that ends a lawsuit without trial in a case where a judge finds that there is no genuine issue of material fact and thus no need to send the matter to a jury.

A last step in a jury trial before the jury begins deliberations, in which the attorneys for both sides sum up the evidence presented and call attention to the important points in their arguments; also called summing up.
A law student who works at a law firm for the summer. See also law, clerk.
To notify a defendant that he or she has been sued and must appear in court on a particular day.
An order issued by a court informing a defendant that a lawsuit has been brought against him or her and summoning him or her to appear in court on a particular day to answer the complaint.
Local or state laws that prohibit certain businesses from operating on Sunday; also called blue laws.
A clause in a statute stating that it will cease to be effective on a certain date unless it is renewed by the legislature.
Laws requiring governmental agencies and departments to open their meetings to the public and make records of those meetings available to the public.
Above; better than; entitled to priority over something else.
A court of general jurisdiction, usually wider jurisdiction than an inferior court, though meaning varies by state.
supremacy.
To take the place of someone or something; to void one thing and replace it with something else.
An intervening cause that substantially affects the ultimate outcome of the series of events and breaks the chain of causation between the direct cause and the effect.
Something that happens in between an action and an injury that interrupts the chain of causation and prevents the initial actor from being liable for the injury.
To occur after an event, in such a way as to change the circumstances.
Something that happens between an action and an injury that changes the circumstances enough that it becomes the cause of the injury.
An employee who oversees the work of other employees.
Something added to something else to improve, enhance, or complete it. V.
A legislative act that clarifies, extends, or completes an existing law.

The discretionary jurisdiction of a federal court to hear and decide matters that are related to claims already within the court's original jurisdiction, such as claims that arise out of the same case or controversy as one already before the court or claims that add parties to the original parties.
An answer, complaint, or other pleading that adds new facts to an original pleading without changing it; see also amend.
A proceeding done after a court has issued judgment against a debtor, to identify the debtor's property and use it to satisfy the judgment; also called supplementary proceeding.

(1) To provide someone with money, a home, and the necessities of life. (2) To encourage; to approve of; to defend; to aid. (3) To corroborate; to back up with evidence. (4) To hold something up; to bear the weight of something. N. support. See also alimony, child support, maintain.

term: Supporting equality and fair treatment for all people
definition: egalitarian.
To end something forcibly; to prevent information from being revealed; to control or restrain. N. suppression.
(1) A court's refusal to admit evidence acquired by unlawful means. (2) A party's refusal or
(Latin) Above; used in a document to refer to something mentioned earlier in the document; see also infra.
Transcending national boundaries, concerns, or governments.
A clause in Article IV of the U.S. Constitution that makes the acts of the federal government the supreme law of the land, superior to all state laws and constitutions.
The highest, most important, or most powerful;
The highest court in a state or in the United States; the court of last resort in many states and in the United States; also used as a name for a court of general jurisdiction in some states.
(1) A charge added on top of a price or payment. (2) A financial penalty imposed on a fiduciary who fails to perform his or her duties properly. V. surcharge.

A person who takes responsibility for someone else's debt, agreeing to pay it if the debtor fails to do so, usually bound by the same original instrument as the debtor and thus responsible for the debt from its inception. See also guarantee.
A federal officer who heads the U.S. Public Health Service; the head of a military medical service.
Excess; something left over after most of an asset or fund has been used for its designated purpose; a corporation's assets remaining after expenses and debts have been paid.

(1) To catch, capture, or discover someone unawares. (2) To astonish or amaze. N. (1) Coming upon someone unawares. (2) An unexpected event; a revelation or situation that a party to a trial has not expected and could not reasonably have prepared for, which potentially can be grounds for a new trial.
To give up; to yield; to hand something over to someone else; for a tenant to give up a lease before its term with the understanding that the landlord will terminate the lease. N. surrender.
The amount that an insurer will pay a policyholder if he or she gives up a life insurance policy, i.e., the policy's current value.
(1) A substitute or deputy. (2) A judicial officer who handles matters such as probate, adoptions, and guardianships at the local level, such as within a county.

A woman who bears a child for another couple, either by having her own egg fertilized with the husband's sperm or by having an embryo made from the couple's gametes implanted in her uterus, and after birth surrenders her parental rights to the couple.
A person other than a child's parent who assumes parental responsibilities toward that child; an advocate appointed for a child by a juvenile court.
An additional tax imposed on something that has already been taxed.
The monitoring or observation of a person or situation, especially done by police pursuing a suspected criminal or gathering evidence of a crime.
surviving.
An action that survives the death of the party who brought it.
A statute that allows the estate of a deceased person to maintain a cause of action for that person's pain and suffering up until the moment of death.
To remain living; to live after someone else has died. N.
A spouse who remains alive after his or her partner has died; see also widow.
A person who remains alive after the death of someone else or after some event.
Becoming entitled to property by outliving someone else who had an interest in it; the right of a joint tenant to take the entire property when his or her co-tenant dies.
suspicious.
A classification of people that seems to have no legitimate purpose, such as grouping people by race or sex when those traits could not be relevant to the matter at hand.
(1) To put on hold; to postpone. (2) To prevent someone from performing a job or attending school for a period of time as a punishment. N. suspension.
A sentence that is not imposed on the defendant on the condition that he or she does not violate terms specified at sentencing. sentences,
A sentence for a criminal offense that is postponed indefinitely unless the convicted person commits another crime within a specified period.
The act of suspecting; a belief that someone has committed a crime or misdeed. V.
(1) To support. (2) To uphold; to affirm the validity of something. (3) To suffer something unpleasant, such as an injury.
To bind oneself or someone else with an oath.
The act of administering an oath to a witness at a trial, to a public official, or to a person undergoing some other formal proceeding. ADJ. sworn.

(1) A contest that awards prizes based on random drawings of entries, often used as a promotion of products. (2) A horse race or other gambling contest in which the winner or winners receive all the stakes placed on the race.
To use fraud to cheat someone out of money. N. swindle, swindling.
A headnote at the beginning of a reported case, summarizing the key legal points contained within.
Conduct or actions without words that express some sentiment or opinion, which may or may not be protected under the First Amendment.

(1) A group of people or companies that form an alliance, permanent or temporary, to conduct some business or activity together. (2) An organization that supplies material to a large number of periodical publications. V. (1) To manage a syndicate; to be managed by a syndicate; to sell something to a syndicate.
adjudication.
rape. rape,
A list or chart that includes many items of information in a concise display. V. To postpone or suspend consideration of something, such as a proposed item of legislation.
A list of citations to all the legal sources used in a brief or other legal document.
An alphabetical list of the cases mentioned in a legal book or document.
Implied but not openly expressed; understood without being explicitly stated.

To add something to something else, such as to add together periods of time in which a property is under adverse possession by different occupants in order to meet the statutory requirement for ownership, to combine coverage from two or more insurance policies in a claim for damages, or to unite two or more liens so as to increase the priority of one of them. N. tacking.
A federal law enacted in 1947 as an addition to the National Labor Relations Act, giving employers increased rights in labor disputes.
A limitation on the ownership of land, usually restricted to one person and his or her heirs. ADJ. Limited as to tenure or ownership. tail,
To grasp or lay hold of something; to seize a person or thing; to steal something; to assume ownership of something; to receive title to property.
To acquire control over or management of a company. N. takeover.
To invoke the Fifth Amendment as justification for refusing to answer a question in a criminal prosecution.

The act of taking something; a government's interference with or regulation of how a property owner can use his or her property in such a way that it substantially impairs the owner's property right. See also condemn, eminent domain, larceny.
To interfere with something so as to change it, especially in a destructive or unauthorized way; to attempt to influence a jury through bribery or other illegal means.
Real; with a physical presence; perceptible to touch and sight. See also intangible.
Evidence that can be seen or touched, as opposed to testimony. tangible property. Physical things, such as land, buildings, and objects, that can be possessed.
A tax or duty imposed on imports and exports; see also customs. tariff,

A charge imposed by the government on people and businesses on various activities and possessions, such as income, property, purchased goods, or inheritances, used by the government to fund itself and its programs. V. (1) To impose a tax. (2) To assess the costs of a legal action.
A rule stating that an amount deducted in an earlier year but then recovered in a later year is to be included in the income of the later year.
A category of income that is subject to a particular tax rate.
A court that has jurisdiction over tax matters.
An amount subtracted from a taxpayer's tax liability.
An amount subtracted from a taxpayer's taxable income.
The crime of paying less in taxes than the amount required by the government, such as by fraudulently reporting a lower income than what was in fact received.
Not subject to taxation.
The percentage of income or purchase price that must be paid in tax; see also tax bracket.
A document filed by a taxpayer with the Internal Revenue Service or state tax agency on which the taxpayer reports a taxable year's income, deductions, and credits, and calculates tax liability.
A fund, transaction, business arrangement, or other method used by a taxpayer to reduce taxable income or tax liability.
Able to be taxed.
The amount of an estate that is subject to taxes, i.e., the gross estate minus any allowable deductions.
A gift that is subject to taxation.
The amount of income subject to taxes, i.e., gross income minus credits and deductions.
A twelve-month period used for calculating taxes; see also fiscal year.
The act of taxing; money paid as tax. See also ad valorem tax, estate tax, gift tax, income tax, payroll tax, sales tax.
The power granted to Congress by the Constitution to levy taxes.
A person or business that pays taxes; the person or entity subject to taxes on a particular transaction.
Using signals to transmit information over a distance, such as over a telephone or over a computer network.
To perform one's job at some location other than the traditional workplace, such as at home, and using information technology to communicate with the workplace.
An employee who performs his or her job at some location other than the traditional workplace, using information technology to communicate with the workplace.
Not permanent; provisional; lasting for a limited time.
Disability due to illness or injury that prevents a person from working for a period of time but that is expected to end when the person recovers.
An order issued by a court forbidding a party to take some action as a temporary measure to protect a plaintiff until a formal hearing on an injunction can take place. ABBRV. TRO.
The condition of being a tenant; an interest in land held by a tenant.
(1) A person who rents property from a landlord. (2) A person who owns or possesses property.
A husband and wife who hold property as joint tenants, with one spouse taking the whole title when the other dies.

Two or more tenants who hold an undivided interest in property but who each have a separate title to a proportional share; when a tenant in common dies, his or her share of the property goes into his or her estate.
To offer or present something to someone; to present someone with money as payment; to offer to perform some service for a price. N. tender. tender,
A seller's actual delivery of goods to a buyer; a seller's placing goods at the buyer's disposal and notifying the buyer of that fact.
An offer to purchase shares of stock, often above market price, in a company made by a person or business that wished to acquire control of the company.
A building, house, dwelling, or other structure affixed to land; a building divided into separate residences that are rented; a dilapidated or run-down apartment building.
(1) A right to hold property; the condition of holding property. (2) The time that a person spends in a position or an

(1) A word or expression for something. (2) The duration of something; a fixed period of time; a period of time in which a court is in session. (3) A condition or requirement in a contract or agreement, usually used in the plural, i.e.,
Able to be terminated.
An interest in property that ends when a particular event occurs or does not occur.
To end; to expire; to fire an employee. N. termination.
To give a name to something.
The rules presented by the creator or producer of a work such as a software application to its users, explaining the rights and obligations that accompany use of the work.
A court in a U.S. territory that functions as both a state and a federal court.
The principle that a state's borders are sacrosanct; the idea in international law that one state or nation should not aid actions that could reform the borders of another state or nation.
A court's or government's power over a specified territory or region.

The waters that are considered the territory of the state off which coast they lie; a band of waters parallel to the coast that can be no more than twelve nautical miles wide. See also Law of the Sea.
All waters contained within a state or country and all ocean waters within a three-mile border around its coast.

A defined area of land; an area or region under the control of one government or ruler; the area in which a judge has jurisdiction; a region that is organized and governed according to the terms of a particular nation but has not yet been admitted to statehood in that nation. ADJ. territorial.
Extreme fear or dread, which can lead to intimidation; the fear typical of someone who is afraid of being hurt or killed.
The use of violence to achieve political ends in order to intimidate citizens and governments into acquiescing to the demands of the terrorists.
A person who engages in terrorism. ADJ. terroristic.
To frighten someone terribly; to use violence or threats to fill someone with dread and alarm.

A lawsuit that serves as a test of the validity of a law, principle, or cause of action; one lawsuit chosen out of many similar ones brought at the same time to be heard by the court and decided as a test of the right of all the similar cases; also called a test action.
A will; the part of a last will and testament that disposes of someone's personal property.
Related to or given by a will.
The mental ability required to compose and execute a valid will, i.e., the ability to understand who one is, what one owns, and what the will will do.
A gift of property from a deceased person to a living one, done through a will.
A testator's true intention in writing a will or naming a recipient of property.
A person who writes and executes a will; a deceased person whose property is disposed of according to the terms of his or her will. FEM. testatrix.
To speak under oath; to give evidence as a witness in a deposition or lawsuit; to serve as proof of something.
The spoken evidence given by a witness under oath in court or at a deposition, or written evidence provided by a witness under oath through an affidavit.
The act of taking something that belongs to someone else, without the owner's permission and with no intention of returning it; stealing; see also larceny, steal.
To create a theory; to generate a possible explanation for some situation.

A set of principles or ideas that serve as the basis or explanation for some action or situation; a generally accepted body of principles based on known facts that explains some circumstance; an idea or premise about something; a legal or factual principle that serves as the basis for a case. See also hypothesis.
interpolation.
Someone who steals.

Thorough and prolonged questioning administered to a criminal suspect by the police with the intention of getting the person to confess to a crime. ADJ. A category of crime less serious than first degree or second degree.
A person who is not directly involved in a transaction; someone who is not a party to an agreement.
A person who is not a party to a contract but who will benefit from it and has rights under it.
A complaint filed by a defendant in an existing lawsuit against someone who is alleged by the defendant to be liable for damages to the plaintiff.
A statement of an intention to harm someone or damage his or her property; a person or thing that seems likely to cause damage or danger; a menace. V. threaten.
A savings and loan or savings bank; also called a thrift.
(1) Duration; how long it takes to do something. (2) A specific moment.
A certificate of deposit; an account from which a depositor must wait a specific period of time before withdrawing.
A phrase used in contracts in which performance by one of the parties at or by a specific time is an essential term. See also reasonable time.
An hourly pay rate commonly paid for overtime work, calculated by adding 50% of the usual hourly rate to the usual hourly rate.
A property, often a vacation home, that is owned by several parties, each of whom is entitled to use it for a specified period of time every year.
A tenth of one's income contributed to something such as a charity or church; in feudal times, the tenth of a farm and other productions that was required to be donated to the church. V. tithe.

(1) A right or claim of ownership of property. (2) The official name of a job or position; the formal address given to a person in a position or job or of particular social status. (3) The name of a book, article, artwork, or other creation. (4) The name of a legislative bill or legal action. V. To name a book or other composition or creation.
A company that checks titles to property in order to find defects and encumbrances, and that also issues title insurance.
Insurance issued by a title company that has investigated the title to a property, guaranteeing that it has no known defects and insuring against claims based on a defective title.
Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, a federal law banning sex discrimination in schools and universities, both academic and athletic.
The official title to a property, found through a title search. See also bad title, cloud on title, good title.

An investigation done by a title company in which it examines the history of a title in the registry of deeds and then prepares an abstract summarizing the transfers of the title and the absence of defects on it.
Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, a federal law that prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of sex, race, religion, or national origin.

A civil settlement in which several major tobacco companies agreed to pay money to the states to pay the health care costs of people injured by smoking and to limit the advertising of tobacco products. to have and to hold. N. A phrase used in a conveyance expressing the fact that the transferor intends for the transferee to receive the property; also used in many marriage ceremonies to convey the parties' intention to be joined to one another.
A payment for the right to use something once, such as the right to drive across a bridge. V. To suspend; to deny or take away.
An advertisement in a newspaper announcing that a corporation is going to put up for sale an offering of stock.
Capacity of a ship; the amount of cargo a ship can carry; the amount of cargo a ship is carrying.
A tax imposed on ships according to tonnage, assessed when they enter a country.

A private injury or wrong; a violation of a socially recognized duty owed to a plaintiff that results in injury to the plaintiff; torts can be caused intentionally, through negligence, or under strict liability. ADJ. tortious.
A federal law that makes the U.S. government liable for damages in tort, waiving sovereign immunity except in specific cases; many states also have tort claims acts.
A person who commits a tort against another.
The infliction of severe mental or physical pain on a person in order to intimidate or punish the victim or to elicit information. V. torture.
The condition of being rendered unable to perform any functions of one's occupation.
A person's inability, due to physical or mental affliction, to perform any work that he or she is qualified to do and that is available in the community.
Complete destruction of property, such that it can no longer be used at all and retains little or no value. to wit. ADV. (Middle English) To know; namely; that is to say.
A test that examines all the circumstances surrounding an event to determine whether a person's constitutional rights have been violated by a search and seizure.
A parcel of land.

(1) Buying and selling goods or services. (2) An exchange; a one-to-one transfer. (3) A skilled job, especially one that involves manual work. (4) A particular kind of business; the people who work in a particular kind of business or profession. V. To buy and sell; to exchange. See also commerce.
An agreement made by two nations regulating trade between them.
commercial.
The appearance and image associated with a particular product, including labels, colors, size, shape, and advertising techniques unique to the product. trade fixture. See fixture.
A name used by a business to identify itself, which stands for the business's reputation and goodwill.
A secret formula, pattern, plan, process, machine, or method of compiling information known and used exclusively by one business that could give competitors an advantage if they acquired it.
A practice or custom accepted by the practitioners of a particular trade that they have every reason to expect to be followed by other practitioners of that trade in a particular

A name, word, symbol, or device used by the manufacturer of a product to identify that product and distinguish it from other similar ones produced by competitors, which can be registered with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office; also called a mark. See also copyright, patent, trade name.
Trade in some commodity; buying, selling, or trading of goods and services, often used regarding trade in illegal commodities such as drugs. V. traffic.
To conduct business; to carry on some activity.
One instance of conducting business, such as buying or selling an item; an agreement or act involving at least two people that alters their legal rights in relation to one another.

A test used to determine whether a cause of action arises out of the same transaction or event that is the subject matter of an existing claim brought by the opposing party, in which case it must be brought as a counterclaim or forever be barred from litigation.
To record spoken words in writing.
An official, certified, written record of a trial or other proceeding prepared by a court reporter.

To move a person or thing from one place to another; to move an object from the possession of one person to the possession of another; to change the title of property from one owner to another; to convey. N. transfer.
A person or company that oversees the sale of shares of publicly traded corporations and keeps records on shareholders.

The practice of transferring goods, services, loans, intellectual property, or other commodities to other jurisdictions or countries in order to set prices at local levels and gain tax advantages. Transfers to Minors Act. See Uniform Transfers to Minors Act.
Able to be transferred.
A person who receives something that is transferred.
Tax liability imposed by the Internal Revenue Service on a person who has received transferred property in cases where it cannot find the transferor who actually owes the taxes.
A person who transfers something to someone else.
A federal agency within the Department of Homeland Security created in 2001 to oversee security of transportation systems within the United States. ABBRV. TSA.
A check purchased from a bank that functions as cash but is protected against loss or theft. See also certified check.
The act of betraying one's country, such as by aiding an enemy of the state or plotting to overthrow the government. ADJ. treasonous.

Treasure found; a cache of money, precious metals, or other valuable items with no known owner found buried in the earth or in a hidden place; the person who finds a treasure trove is entitled to it before anyone except the true owner.
A place where wealth is stored, especially public funds; the branch of the government responsible for saving and distributing revenue.
A promissory note issued by the U.S. Treasury with a maturity period of one year or less, which yields no interest but is sold at a discount and paid in full on maturity.
(1) A bond issued by the U.S. Treasury that matures in a term greater than five years. (2) A bond issued and then bought back by the same corporation.
A note issued by the U.S. Treasury for an intermediate term, i.e., maturing in one to five years, which pays interest by coupon.
Stock issued and then later repurchased by a corporation.
A formal agreement between two or more nations concerning some matter of public welfare.
A clause in the Constitution that gives the president the power to make treaties with other nations and the Senate the right to approve them.
An award of damages that is tripled as further punishment for the wrongdoer.
To enter or disturb property owned by someone else without the owner's permission. N. trespass. trespass,
A common law action that allowed someone to recover for injury that resulted indirectly from a defendant's actions; also called case.
A person who trespasses.

A formal judicial proceeding in which a judge and sometimes a jury hear the evidence in a case and decide the rights of the parties in a civil case or the guilt or innocence of the defendant in a criminal case. See also bench trial, jury trial.
The court in which a case is first presented, as opposed to an appellate court.
A judicial court; a judge's seat or bench; a judge or group of judges with jurisdiction in an area.
To triple; to multiply by three.
Temporary restraining order.
Refusal to attend school by a minor required to attend, without permission or justification.
A student who refuses to attend school.
Accurate; reliable; in agreement with the facts; genuine;
An indictment; the endorsement of an indictment by the grand jury who has made it.
A copy of a document that is not necessarily an exact duplicate of the original but is close enough to be clearly understood and recognized.
The fair market value of property.
(1) Confidence that someone will act in a particular way; firm belief in someone's reliability. (2) A legal arrangement in which one person or company (the trustee) holds title to property
A company that acts as a trustee for people who want to create trusts.
Property or money held in a trust, administered by a trustee and intended to benefit someone who does not own it.
A document containing the terms and conditions governing a trust.
The person who holds the property of a trust and administers it in a fiduciary capacity for the benefit of the beneficiaries.
A person who handles the administration of bankruptcies, managing the financial affairs of the bankrupt person or company and paying creditors.
A person who creates a trust; a settlor.
An inmate in a correctional institution who, due to good behavior, is allowed a degree of freedom and responsibility denied to other prisoners.
A federal law requiring lenders to reveal to consumers applying for commercial credit the true cost of that credit, in order to enable consumers to shop around for the most favorable credit terms.
To examine a case in a judicial trial; to subject a criminal suspect to a trial.
Transportation Security Administration.

A building contract in which the contractor assumes all risks and performs all work, leaving the owner of the project nothing to do toward its construction but "turn the key" to open the door once it is finished.
The rate of replacement of goods, funds, or personnel; in a company, the rate at which employees leave and are replaced by others.
Another term for "attractive nuisance," socalled because railroad turntables are known to attract children who can be harmed by playing on them. turpitude. See moral turpitude.
A seller's refusal to sell a buyer a desired item unless the buyer also purchases a specific other item, whether the buyer wants the additional item or not.
Rule by a tyrant.
Uniform Commercial Code.
Uniform Consumer Credit Code.
Uniform Gifts to Minors Act. See Uniform Transfers to Minors Act.
Unit investment trust.
The facts that ultimately form the basis of the decision in a case.
(Latin) Beyond the powers; outside one's official authority; used especially to describe actions by a corporation that exceed the powers granted to it by its charter or by state law.
Extremely dangerous; involving a serious risk of harm to people or property that cannot be avoided no matter how careful one is.
An activity that is so abnormally dangerous that no amount of care can prevent risk of harm, and that therefore gives rise to strict liability.
United Nations.

term: Unable to be reformed or corrected
definition: A person who refuses to be reformed; an unmanageable person, especially a juvenile.

To state definitely. expressio unius est exclusio alterius. (Latin) That which is expressed is included and that which is not is excluded; a rule of statutory interpretation that says that if something is mentioned as being included, then anything that is not mentioned is by implication meant not to be included.
Agreed to by all concerned parties without a negative vote or opinion. N. unanimity.
Done without official authorization or permission.
Using an automobile without permission of the owner.
Impossible to avoid or prevent.
An accident that could not have been prevented by the parties involved in it, no matter what steps they took to avoid it, and not caused by their negligence or lack of skill or care.
Not yet born; can refer to a fetus in the womb or a child who has not yet been conceived.
doubt. See also reasonable doubt.
The equitable doctrine that a plaintiff's own fault must be considered when determining the remedy he or she should receive from a defendant. See also clean hands doctrine.
The citizenry; the community; all people.
Unreasonably detrimental; grossly one-sided; so unfair or oppressive to the interests of a party to a contract as to render the contract unenforceable. N. unconscionability.
Conflicting with the Constitution of the United States or another constitution.
Not able to be challenged or contested.
A clause in a life insurance policy that says the insurer may not contest a claim after the policy has been in force for a specified period of time.
Describes the state of a person after swearing an oath.
Of a vehicle, able to be stopped quickly when necessary.
Unwillingly; done under compulsion while making one's unwillingness known; used to describe an action done unwillingly and with objection.
A company controlled by another company that owns a majority of the shares of its stock; also called a subsidiary corporation.
Intoxicated; suffering from mental and physical impairment due to consumption of alcohol or drugs.
(1) To insure; to accept the liability for something. (2) To agree to buy all unsold shares from an issue of stocks or
A person, bank, insurance company, or other institution that underwrites something.
Not divided or broken up into portions.
An interest in property held by tenants in common, joint tenants, or tenants by the entirety, each of whom has a right to use the entire property; also called an undivided right; see also tenant.
Improper; unwarranted; exceeding what is necessary.

Excessive or improper influence exerted on a person writing a will or giving away property by someone who wants to take away the person's free will and dominate the action; influence that interferes with someone's free will or judgment and subjugates it to someone else's wishes.
Not earned.
Interest paid to someone by a financial institution before it is actually earned.
All surplus that is not earned, including surplus from the revaluation of assets, surplus from contributions by shareholders or others, and paid-in surplus. See also income, unearned.
Insurance that pays benefits to temporarily unemployed workers; in the United States, an insurance program that federal law requires each state to fund and administer.
Not ethical; not according to morals or the ethical standards of a profession.

The tort of fraudulently trying to pass off one's goods as those of another company, or to use the reputation of another company to sell one's own product, as through misleading advertising or packaging.

Acts forbidden to both employers and employees under the National Labor Relations Act, including for a union to coerce employees and employers, or to refuse to bargain, or for an employer to interfere with the employees' attempts to exercise their rights.
Not suitable for a particular purpose; not qualified; incompetent.
Conforming everywhere to the same standard; remaining the same everywhere; applying equally to everyone concerned.
A compilation of laws governing the conduct of military personnel.
A compilation of laws governing commercial transactions, including the sale of goods, commercial paper, banking, and secured transactions. ABBRV. UCC.
A law adopted by some states that regulates consumer credit. ABBRV. UCCC. Uniform Gifts to Minors Act. See Uniform Transfers to Minors Act. ABBRV. UGMA.
Compilations of laws that address various subject areas and attempt to make the law in those areas uniform, such as
A commonly used synonym for Uniform Resource Locator. ABBRV. URI.

The series of characters that will retrieve a particular web page when typed into the address bar in a Web browser; a domain name or IP address plus other information that specifies a particular page. ABBRV. URL.
A guide to legal citation published by the Harvard Law Review Association and accepted as the standard guide to citation; synonymous with Blue Book.

An act governing transfers of property to a trust managed by a custodian for the benefit of named minors; an act called the Uniform Gifts to Minors Act was the original law of this type, but the Uniform Transfers to Minors Act has replaced it in most states. ABBRV. UTMA.
One-sided; performed by one party in a situation without the participation or agreement of other parties; affecting only one party or group in a particular situation. See also contract, unilateral.
Without insurance coverage.
Insurance that covers the insured for injury or damage caused by a driver who does not have liability insurance.

(1) A group of states or nations with a central government. (2) The act of joining together; a state of togetherness or harmony. (3) A labor organization that exists to negotiate with employers on behalf of workers in matters such as wages, hours, and workplace safety.
To become a member of a labor union or persuade others to join it. ADJ. unionized.
A registered trust that purchases fixedincome securities and holds them to maturity, sold to investors in units (typically of $1,000 each). ABBRV. UIT.
A marijuana cigarette.

term: United by a treaty or common agreement
definition: An accomplice.
An international organization founded in 1945 to help nations cooperate in matters of human rights, economics, security, and international law. ABBRV. UN.

term: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
definition: A nonbinding international treaty passed in 1992 that states the desirability of reducing greenhouse gas emissions and slowing climate change. See also Kyoto Protocol.
An attorney appointed by the president to represent the U.S. government in civil lawsuits and other matters in a judicial district; see also district attorney.

term: United States Citizenship and Immigration Services
definition: A bureau in the Department of Homeland Security that oversees immigration, naturalization, visa applications, and petitions for asylum. See also Immigration and Naturalization Services. ABBRV. USCIS.
A compilation of federal statutes,
The annotated version of the USC, supplemented with case notes, cross-references, and historical references. ABBRV. USCA.
The principles according to which the federal government of the United States is organized, enacted in 1787 and modified by twenty-seven amendments.

term: United States Court of Federal Claims
definition: The federal court that hears claims against the U.S. government; this court replaced the Court of Claims in 1982. Synonymous with Claims Court, U.S.
The government service that collects customs duties, seizes contraband, processes people and goods coming in and out of the United States, and enforces applicable trade statutes.

The department of the federal government that handles financial policy, collects taxes, issues currency, manages public debt, and oversees several bureaus including the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, the Secret Service, and the Internal Revenue Service.

term: United States Department of Veterans Affairs
definition: A federal agency that provides benefits and services to military veterans. ABBRV. VA.
An officer appointed by the president for a judicial district who executes all writs and orders issued by federal courts.
An administrative agency that functions as a court to hear disputes over amounts of income, estate, and gift taxes between taxpayers and the Internal Revenue Service. unities,
The state of forming a united whole; agreement or harmony between people.
Sharing an interest in the same property.
Holding equal rights to use and possess the same property.
Sharing an interest in property simultaneously.
Receiving an interest in property through the same conveyance or instrument.
Not just; unfair.
Knowingly receiving and accepting a benefit without paying for it or a benefit that rightfully belongs to someone else.
Against the law; illegal.
The gathering of three or more people with the intention of disturbing the peace or committing some unlawful act; definition varies by state.
The act of keeping possession of a property by someone who once had a right to it but no longer does, such as a tenant whose lease has ended but who refuses to leave.
Entering property without the owner's consent through the use of force or fraud.
A security that is not listed on a stock exchange and can only be traded over the counter.
Sexual acts that were historically considered unnatural or wrong, including sodomy or buggery; see also crime against nature.

A condition found when the physical characteristics of a piece of property prevent it from being used for purposes permitted by zoning laws, or when it would be prohibitively expensive to use it in the permitted ways, so zoning laws are therefore found to impose unreasonable difficulties on the property owner and a variance is warranted.
Not achieved; not converted into money.
Profit or loss that has not yet been converted into actual money, existing only in principle or "on paper," such as a rise or loss in stock prices.
Arbitrary; excessive; unfair; irrational. unreasonable search and seizure. See search and seizure.
Unasked-for; not requested or sought after.
Uniform Resource Identifier.
Uniform Resource Locator.

Uniting and Strengthening America by Providing Appropriate Tools Required to Intercept and Obstruct Terrorism Act of 2001; a law passed shortly after September 11, 2001, that strengthened U.S. law enforcement agencies for the purpose of preventing future terrorist attacks.
A practice that is usual within a particular business; the typical way of doing an activity or interpreting a word or phrase within a profession or kind of business. See also custom, trade usage.
United States Code.
United States Code Annotated.
United States Citizenship and Immigration Services.
To employ someone or something; to put into action; to enjoy the benefits of real or personal property. N. The act of using; the fact of being used; the right to enjoy the benefits of property.
To take someone's fingerprint.
Able to be put to some beneficial use.
For tax purposes, the period during which an asset is expected to be useful to its owner before wearing out or otherwise becoming useless.
A person who uses something.

term: usually an improper or illegal way
definition: Money or a gift given to influence someone.

term: usually due to nonconformity or untrustworthiness
definition: blacklist.
fabrication.
(1) A protected body of water for anchoring ships. (2) A safe place; a refuge.
To imprison; to put someone in jail. See also prison, correctional institution.
(1) An action or deed. (2) A law or written ordinance passed by Congress or another legislative body; when done by Congress, this is called an act of Congress.
The civil law right to use property that belongs to someone else.
A person who lends money at unreasonably high interest.
To take over; to supplant; to take over a position of power by force. N. usurpation.
A person who usurps a position.
Uniform Transfers to Minors Act.
The greatest amount or extent possible. ADJ. utmost.
Highest care; the care that a very cautious and competent person would use.
The highest degree of resistance a person is capable of, historically required of a victim of rape in order to charge the offender with rape.
United States Department of Veterans Affairs.
(1) To leave; to make empty. (2) To make void; to annul.
A break from work; leave from work to be used for any purpose, particularly recreation.
The act of living as a vagrant.
A person with no regular job or home who wanders from place to place and supports himself or herself by begging.
Uncertain; unclear;
Properly executed and legally binding; legitimate;
To make valid.
Worth money; important.
Something of value given in exchange for a performance or a promise, which entitles the recipient of the promise to enforce a claim against a promisor who fails to perform.

(1) Worth; cost or price in money; the price a seller would give a buyer in a bona fide transaction in an open market. (2) Importance; utility. (3) A moral principle. V. (1) To estimate the worth of something. (2) To consider important.
A person who willfully destroys or damages property.
The act of willfully destroying or damaging property. V. vandalize.

(1) A discrepancy. (2) A disagreement between the allegations in a pleading and the evidence offered to prove them. (3) Permission to use property in a way not allowed under applicable zoning ordinances; see also unnecessary hardship.
A unique seventeen-digit number that identifies any given motor vehicle. ABBRV. VIN.
Of or related to motor vehicles, such as automobiles and motorcycles. See also homicide, vehicular.
To sell.
A buyer.
A seller.
(Latin) To come; the writ that summons potential jurors to court; the group of potential jurors for a case.
A risky undertaking; a business enterprise that involves some risk. V. To dare to do something; to undertake some enterprise that involves risk of loss.
Funding for a new business that involves risk but has potential for great profits. See also joint venture.
The place where something happens; a court that has jurisdiction over a matter; a court in which cases from a particular location may be brought; the district in which a federal lawsuit is brought.
Truth; honesty; accuracy.
Expressed in words; oral; spoken rather than written.
A jury's or judge's finding on a question of fact, to be used by the court in determining its final judgment.
To confirm the truth, accuracy, or authenticity of something; to attest to the truth of something in an affidavit or other sworn statement. N. verification.
(Latin) Against; used in the title of a lawsuit to indicate which party is against which. ABBRV. v. or vs.
Price fixing done between levels of production, such as between retailer and manufacturer, manufacturer and supplier, etc.
To accrue; to take effect; to bestow on someone; to give someone authority, power, or a right; to gain a legal right to something; to come into possession of something.
Fixed; settled; secure in someone's possession; having the right to present or future enjoyment; established or protected by law or contract.
A property interest currently in someone's possession or that will certainly come into his or her possession in the future, and therefore can be given away or sold by him or her.
A gift given at present, or a gift that will certainly take effect in the future and that is not contingent on some condition.
An interest that is recognized as belonging to someone who has the right to give it away; a personal stake in some matter.

A pension to which an employee is unconditionally entitled because he or she has completed the required period of employment, whether or not he or she is working for that particular employer at the time of retirement.
In constitutional law, a right that is so firmly attached to a person that it cannot be taken away by someone else.
An employee's acquisition of an unconditional right to receive retirement benefits after working for an employer for the required period.
A person who has served in the armed forces and been honorably discharged.
The act of rejecting a law or decision that has been passed by a legislature, usually done by a president or governor who must sign it in order for it to become valid. veto,
Troublesome; annoying; worrisome.
A lawsuit brought without probable cause, out of malice toward the opposing party.
(1) Feasible; able to work. (2) Of a fetus, capable of living outside its mother's womb, with or without artificial life support. N. viability.
Experienced or done through another person.
Liability imposed on one person for the actions of another based on a relationship between them, such as that between a principal and an agent or a parent and child.
