Immorality; bad behavior; a bad habit. PREFIX. Next in rank; a deputy for someone else, such as a vice president.
A crime involving immorality, such as drug use or prostitution.

term: Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties
definition: An international treaty effective in 1980 that codified and clarified accepted international law on the creation of treaties.
Vehicle identification number.
An immoral, unjust, or injurious act; a violation of someone's legal rights that results in harm; a breach of one's legal duty that results in harm to someone else.
Using physical force, especially unwarranted force used to intimidate or physically defeat someone else,
A crime involving physical force, such as assault or murder.
Death caused by a violent external force, such as another person.
Almost but not quite as described or intended; effective for all practical purposes, though not officially acknowledged.
The condition of being represented in a lawsuit without being named as a party by a party who shares the same interest, with the nonparty agreeing to be bound by the judgment.
A computer program that can copy itself and install itself onto a computer without help from or the knowledge of the computer's owner or user.
A document or stamp issued by a country to a foreign national, giving the holder the right to enter the country for a specified period and for specified purposes.
A formally organized meeting with someone, especially the time that a divorced spouse is allowed to spend with the children from his or her marriage.
Rights granted to divorced parents without custody to visit their children, and occasionally granted to grandparents as well.
To ruin something; to void or annul; to impair something's effectiveness.
(Latin) Videlicet, meaning obviously, namely, or of course; in other words; used to explain or rephrase preceding words in order to clarify them.
A recording of someone's voice made during a criminal investigation, to be used as a basis for comparison against recorded evidence.
Invalid; not legally binding; ineffective; empty. See also valid. v. To declare something legally invalid.
Never having had legal validity; void from the beginning.
Describes a statute so obscure, difficult to understand, or unclear that it is impossible for ordinarily intelligent people to agree on a meaning, and so it must be void.
Describes a statute or instrument that has such obvious errors or imperfections that it must be invalid,
Able to be annulled or voided due to some irregularity or problem, but valid until that happens.
A contract that may be legally voided by one or both parties, but that is valid until voided.

(French) To say the truth. (1) The preliminary examination of potential jurors by the judge and attorneys to decide whether they are qualified for jury service and whether any conflicts exist. (2) A hearing done during a trial outside the hearing of the jury in order to determine some point that the court must rule on without the jury.
Done willingly, without the influence of or pressure from someone else.
An indication of a choice for candidates in an election; an individual ballot or other means of indicating such choice submitted by a voter; the right to vote.
A person who votes; a person who has the right to vote.

A federal law passed in 1965 that guarantees all citizens the right to vote without discrimination by forbidding districts to require people to meet conditions such as literacy tests or educational requirements.
A device in which corporate shareholders transfer their voting rights to a trustee who votes for them in corporate elections in order to pool their votes and control corporate affairs.
To verify; to confirm that a person is of good character or qualified for a position; to assert that something is true, based on personal experience.
A small printed document that entitles its holder to some good or service; a receipt; a document that authorizes the payment of cash.
A form filled out by an employee informing the employer how many tax deductions the employee may take and thus how much the employer should withhold.
The 1973 U.S. Supreme Court case that held that abortions are permissible for any reason up to the point of the fetus' viability.

Compensation for work performed, particularly pay by the hour or by production paid weekly or daily to a manual or skilled worker; often called wages. See also salary. V. To carry on some enterprise, particularly a war. wage,
A bet; an agreement made by two parties that one of them will pay the other a specific sum if a certain condition occurs. V. wager.
A period of time that must elapse before a person can take some action or enforce some legal right.
To voluntarily give up a legal right or claim.
The act of intentionally and voluntarily surrendering a right or claim; a document that records a person's surrender of a right or claim.
(1) Reckless; malicious; deliberate, unprovoked, and in disregard of the safety of others. (2) Immodest; sexually promiscuous.
During wartime, a violation of the laws of war. See also Geneva Conventions.
(1) A territorial division in a town or city. (2) An area of a hospital or prison. (3) A person, especially a child, under the care and supervision of a guardian.
A person who is under the care of the court, or for whom the court has appointed a guardian.

A building used to store goods. V. (1) To store goods in a warehouse. (2) For a mortgage broker to retain a mortgage until the mortgage resale market improves. (3) For a corporation to notify certain investors of a tender offer before it is made public, allowing those investors to purchase stock at a low price.
A document issued by a person who runs a warehouse, indicating that the goods have been deposited into his or her care, which serves as a document of title for its holder.

A written order issued by some authority directing someone to do a certain act, particularly an order issued by the state directing a law enforcement officer to arrest someone. V. (1) To justify; to make necessary. (2) To guarantee or confirm; to affirm that a title is good; to confirm that something is as it is represented. See also search warrant. warrant,
A person who receives a warrant.
An arrest made without a warrant, occasionally justifiable if the arresting officer has sufficient probable cause.
A person who makes a warranty.

A promise or assurance that something is true, of a certain quality, or useful for a particular purpose; an assurance by one party to a contract that a certain fact is true, given to save the other party the trouble of confirming it, which is in effect a promise to cover any losses suffered by the other party if the fact turns out not to be true. warranty,
A deed that contains a warranty of good title.

term: warranty of fitness for a particular purpose
definition: A promise by the seller, expressed or implied, that the purchased item will be suitable for the use to which the buyer wishes to put it.
An assurance, expressed or implied, given by a landlord to a new tenant that the premises are suitable for human habitation.
An implied assurance by a seller that the goods he or she sells are fit for the general purpose for which they are sold.
An international convention that regulates international air transport of people and goods.

(1) A sale of stock followed by a purchase of the same stock within a short period of time, usually thirty days; losses on such a sale and repurchase may not be recognized for tax purposes. (2) Simultaneous buying and selling of a stock issue to
The sale of a security at a loss and the repurchase of the same security within thirty days before or after the sale, which does not result in a capital loss for tax purposes; a wash sale.

(1) The act of using something carelessly for no good reason. (2) The things left over from producing something, which have no value and must be disposed of. (3) Damage to or neglect of property by a life tenant that reduces the property's value. V. waste.
A natural resource that is depleted as it is used and that eventually will cease to be useful, such as a coal mine; also called wasting property.

Stock issued by a corporation whose total worth is less than its capital investment; stock issued for less than adequate consideration, as a bonus or as part of compensation, which means that its true value is actually less than its stated market value.

(1) A transparent mark on a piece of paper that can be seen when the paper is held to a light, used as a sign of genuineness. (2) A mark on the bank of a body of water that indicates the highest or lowest point to which the water has risen or dropped.
A road, path, or passage. See also right of way.
Raising money for governmental purposes.
An object designed or used to hurt or kill, either offensively or defensively.
A weapon that can kill or injure a very large number of people simultaneously and destroy an
See World Wide Web.
The visits a website receives from users.
The individual responsible for administering and maintaining a website.
A collection of documents, images, and other forms of digital information on one computer that broadcasts them on the Internet.
The preponderance of the evidence; the side of the case that has more evidence supporting it.
(1) Well-being, quality of life. (2) Financial aid paid by the government to the needy.
General physical and mental well-being.
A privately owned online legal research system that provides access to reported decisions, federal and state statutes, law review articles, and many other materials; see also LexisNexis.

An area of land on the edge of a body of water, where aquatic and terrestrial life forms intersect. when issued. ADV. Shortened form of phrase "when, as, and if issued," used to describe a security that has not yet been authorized for issue, or that has been authorized but does not in fact exist yet, which can be purchased in a conditional transaction that becomes complete when the stock is officially issued.
(1) Considering that something is the case; used in legal documents to begin an introductory statement. (2) In comparison to; in contrast with.
An employee who reports wrongdoing by his or her employer or coworkers.
Federal and state laws that prevent employers from retaliating against employees who report wrongdoing in the workplace. WHO. ABBRV. World Health Organization.
A crime usually done by professionals and businesspeople, such as extortion, computer crime, wire fraud, and government contract fraud. See also felony, misdemeanor.
The selling of goods, usually in large quantities, to retailers who will in turn sell them to consumers.
A person or company who buys large quantities of goods from manufacturers or suppliers, and then sells them to retailers.
A wife who remains alive after her husband dies.
Property and money that a widow can claim from her husband's estate for her own support.
See election by spouse.
A website that can be edited by any user.

A strike not authorized or organized by a labor union, usually illegal. will. N. (1) Desire; intention; choice. (2) A document in which a person describes how to distribute his or her property after death. V. To leave property to someone through a will. See also holograph, last will and testament, living will, testament.
Deliberate; intentional; premeditated.
Conduct that displays an intentional disregard of one's legal duties or of the safety of others.
Concluding the affairs of a corporation or partnership that is being liquidated, including paying off debts and distributing the remaining assets.
World Intellectual Property Organization.
Using an electronic device to eavesdrop on telephone conversations or other communications; see also eavesdrop, pen register.
To retreat; to remove or take away; to take money out of an account. N. withdrawal.

To hold something back; to keep something that is desired by someone else; to deduct income tax from an employee's pay and send it to the Internal Revenue Service on the employee's behalf. N. withholding. with prejudice. ADV. Describes a claim or action that has been dismissed and that may not be brought again as a new action. without prejudice. ADV. With no legal rights affected; describes a claim or action that may be brought as a new action after dismissal. without recourse. ADJ. (1) Having no further rights or possible remedies. (2) Nonrecourse.

A person who has seen or experienced some event; a person who testifies under oath about seeing or experiencing some event, or about the character of someone involved in a lawsuit. See also character witness, material witness.
A federal statute that provides for the protection of witnesses in cases involving organized crime.
The right of women to vote in political elections, granted by the Nineteenth Amendment in 1920.
A word that has a particular meaning when used in a particular field, such as law or medicine, and that has a different meaning or no meaning when used in ordinary speech; also called term of art.

A copyrightable work prepared either by an employee in the course of employment, or by someone commissioned to do the work, on the condition that the person who prepares the material is paid for the job and does not retain any rights to it, including copyright. The employer or commissioner of the work is considered the author for copyright purposes.
Documents, notes, memoranda, and other materials prepared by an attorney as part of representing a client, especially in anticipation of litigation on a particular matter.
A rule protecting work product from discovery.
A program that allows an inmate in a correctional institution to go out to work during the day and return to the institution at night.
A person's combined work and nonwork activities.
A system of benefits provided by employers to employees who are injured on the job.

Federal and state laws that regulate the awards given by employers to employees injured on the job, generally holding that an employer is strictly liable for all injuries regardless of the employee's fault, and preventing employees who receive workers' compensation from filing civil lawsuits in an attempt to win damages.
A financial institution that exists to reduce global poverty by providing loans to help poor nations develop.
An agency of the United Nations that works to promote international public health. ABBRV. WHO.
An agency of the United Nations that promotes creative activity and the protection of intellectual property. ABBRV. WIPO.
An international organization that regulates trade among nations. ABBRV. WTO.
The body of interlinked documents on websites, accessible through the Internet by using a Web browser. Synonymous with Web.
Monetary value; the quality of something. See also net worth.

A second mortgage combined with an older mortgage with a lower interest rate than the current one, which results in a new interest rate between the old and current mortgages; the borrower makes payments to the new lender, who in turn makes payments to the lender that provided the original mortgage.
A written command issued by a court or other authority ordering someone to do a particular act.
An equitable remedy that transfers title to property that has been determined by the court to belong to the recipient.
A court order to seize a debtor's property; see also attach. writ of certiorari. See certiorari.

A writ issued by an appellate court to a lower court ordering it to send the appellate court the record of a case tried in the lower court so that the appellate court can review it for errors in application of the law.
A writ ordering a law enforcement officer to enforce a judgment by the court.
An order issued by a higher court to a lower one preventing it from exceeding its jurisdiction.
The act of writing; a written document or instrument.
Something set down in writing; see also instrument.
(1) Incorrect; false. (2) Immoral; dishonest;
A person who violates someone's rights or injures someone else.
Causing injury; infringing on someone's rights; reckless; unfair.
An act that infringes on someone's rights and injures him or her.
A common law rule, discarded by some jurisdictions, requiring that death occur within one year and one day of some wrongful act for that act to be considered murder.
An employment contract that threatens to fire an employee if he or she joins a labor union, prohibited under most laws and unenforceable in court.
(1) To produce; to deliver. (2) To surrender; to give up. N. That which is produced by something; the money produced from an investment. yield,
The return that an investor will receive from a bond held until it matures.

A criminal who is older than a juvenile but still young, usually between the ages of 18 and 25, who might receive special consideration at sentencing to maximize chances of rehabilitation. year-and-a-day rule 548
